programme.title: The Fugitive

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate when three dehydrated survivors from a helicopter crash are discovered. The pilot is initially missing, but his body later washes ashore with a gunshot wound to the head. Things get complicated when one of the crew members tells the agents the chopper was hit by another aircraft, while another states that the pilot tried to kill them all by crashing into the sea on purpose. American drama, starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The driver of a Ferrari is gunned down by a motorcyclist and the victim is identified as petty officer Colin Boxer, who was holding down a second job as a valet parker and had borrowed the sports car. The owner of the vehicle turns out to be Vijay Chaya, the billionaire head of a large corporation, and the team suspects a case of mistaken identity. During the course of the investigation, Gibbs chances on a medal awarded to Leroy Jethro Moore, the man he was named after and an estranged friend of his father. Guest starring Billy Dee Williams (The Empire Strikes Back) and Ralph Waite (The Waltons)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. First lieutenant Michael Torres gets involved in a fight before falling to the ground, and is found beaten to death the next morning. The only witness is his friend and colleague Captain Joe Westcott, who suffers from post-traumatic stress disorder and has trouble distinguishing reality from his past military experiences in the Middle East

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Tony reviews satellite footage of rebel forces in the Middle East and spots Kersey among a group of insurgents, concluding that Joe Westcott's fear of him was well-founded. Back at HQ, Abby creates a 3D computer model of the attack that occurred in Afghanistan and Joe dons a virtual-reality headset to relive the traumatic events. Will it jog his memory?

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Teenager Lydia Wade manages to escape a gang of masked men trying to abduct her and her friend Rosie, the daughter of a US Navy commander. Lydia's Navy captain father David Wade was shot dead during the attack and her mother is overseas, so Ziva offers to look after the girl, while Abby tries to jog her memory by creating facial reconstructions of the attackers based on some grainy CCTV footage. DiNozzo and McGee find links on Rosie's laptop to possible suspect John 'Diesel' Stevens

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team searches for a top-secret CIA aircraft when two agents end up in Area 51 during a routine mission, while Walter plans to upload Megan's consciousness onto a hard drive

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony is forced to tackle a case with a personal element when his father is discovered drunk in a car that has a dead body in the boot. The victim is revealed to be Navy lieutenant Dean Massey, who DiNozzo Sr had threatened to kill after being cut out of a land development deal. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Navy captain Jake Marsden is murdered in his hotel room and inquiries lead to marine lieutenant Emma Reynolds - who tells the team she was the intended target. After being stationed in Kabul, she became pregnant by a local man called Amir who was later killed in action - and Abby discovers he was the sole male heir of a wealthy family that owns a large tract of land in a politically sensitive part of Afghanistan

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a Navy commander last seen in the company of a mysterious blonde woman, who turns out to be missing NCIS agent and Tony's ex-lover EJ Barrett. She identifies the murderer as Seal-turned-assassin Marcellus Dane, who is suspected of working for Casey Stratton - a member of black ops unit Phantom Eight. Guest starring Sarah Jane Morris

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An estate agent and two clients discover the body of Lt Com Maya Burris, a naval attache who had been working in Egypt, and her detective husband Nick arrives on the scene to demand answers. Using documents discovered on the victim's boat, Abby links her to Barry Norton, who is suspected of having sold government secrets to a Middle Eastern terrorist group known as the SRC

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs goes into his favourite diner for a cup of coffee and finds himself staring down the barrel of a gun, forcing him to question choices he has made in the past and present. The action then flashes back a few days to when he and the rest of the team members were investigating the death of a petty officer on board USS Gentry, which has been undergoing repairs in dry dock. The 200th episode of the crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A missing US Navy lieutenant's car is found covered in blood, and the team discovers she had been profiling men to find the perfect partner. When further investigations reveal she had planned to attend a seminar at a local hotel, Ziva goes undercover in a bid to find out more. Meanwhile, Jenny makes Tony a secret offer

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The shooting of a petty officer looks like an open-and-shut case when a Russian man is found with a recently fired gun in his car. However, the investigation takes a dramatic turn when it is revealed he is working as a spy for the Department of Homeland Security. Military crime drama, with Mark Harmon, Cote de Pablo and David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a lance corporal, whose body has been found in an empty house, and discovers he had a number of spurned lovers and a $2million inheritance. Ducky and Gibbs interview one of the dead marine's partners, but disagree over whether she is telling the truth. David McCallum and Mark Harmon star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Kidnappers strike on Halloween, wounding a marine sergeant and snatching his daughter - leaving the team members wondering why anyone would try to extort money from someone on such a modest salary. As the agents begin searching for a motive, Abby surprises everyone by turning up at the lab in a revealing fancy-dress costume

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team joins forces with military investigators to ascertain whether terrorists were responsible for an explosion at a golf course in which a marine colonel was killed. As inquiries proceed, McGee has to use his computer skills to break into secret government files to ensure the murderers are apprehended. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Eric pulls the team together when he finds a video of kidnapped agent Dominic Vail posted on a jihadist website, with the terrorists holding him stating that he will be executed within 24 hours unless a comrade of theirs being held by the US government is released. One of the captors is identified as a being an associate of Sam, and his involvement also seems to indicate Dom is being held in Sudan. Crime drama, starring LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: An attack on a private plane immediately after it lands turns out to be the hijack of the secret army transfer of terrorist leader Ala A Din Keshwar, the man whose release was demanded in exchange for Dom's life. Callen and the rest of the NCIS team vow to get their hands on Keshwar before he can flee the country. Meanwhile, Hetty offers her resignation to Vance following Dom's death

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen's cover is blown while he is working undercover and he sends a coded text to Hetty, who effectively cuts him off from NCIS as she makes it the team's top priority to discover who found out his real identity and how. A man called Eugene Keelson contacts Callen and threatens to reveal information about his past unless he runs an errand for him, which involves delivering a flash drive to Bulgarian gang. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Keelson is found to have had a huge file labelled 'Callen, G', but it is destroyed remotely before Callen can retrieve it. The team is assigned to monitor Iranian terrorists trying to get their hands on a huge hidden fortune that belonged to the former shah, and the case appears to have a concrete link to Callen when a woman in the group's ranks is identified as being his sister Amy. Starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.desc: The bodies of five people, all of whom are identified as being from the US Navy, are found on the floor of a diner, and all are connected to a suspicious tax firm that specialises in helping people who are in arrears. When further killings are linked to the business, the team realises the crime seem to be copycats of previous cases. Meanwhile, two of Gibbs' ex-wives appear at the location with something important to discuss with the agent. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

Vance brings in Tony's ex-fiancee, journalist Wendy Miller, who claims the victim had been trying to expose a scandal, and as the team pursues the case, a real-estate scam is uncovered. Guest starring Perrey Reeves (Entourage)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The officers race against time to track down a highly specialised hit squad that has murdered an agent on assignment in Cyprus, desperate to intervene before a second target is killed. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: American social worker Sarah Goode is taken hostage by militants in northern Syria, and thanks to the efforts of the Navy Seals, is eventually returned home unharmed. Evidence from the incident suggests US citizen Kyle Nasry was connected to the kidnapping, but when the agents trace Nasry to a rally held by a controversial imam, they are surprised to find Sarah among the attendees. Guest starring Julian Fellowes and featuring Alexander Armstrong in an early role

programme.title: Doctor Who

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part one of three. Captain Jack Harkness throws himself back into the Doctor's life as the Time Lord tries to stop the Tardis hurtling out of control. Eventually the ship lands on a distant planet at the end of the universe, where a lonely professor is trying to save his people from extinction. Derek Jacobi (Gladiator) guest stars, with John Barrowman reprising his role from the first series and spin-off show Torchwood

programme.title: The 39 Steps
programme.desc: Rupert Penry-Jones stars in this adaptation of John Buchan's classic novel, set in 1914. Engineer Richard Hannay is bored and restless in London - until he meets Scudder, who claims to be a British spy. When the agent is killed in his flat, Hannay is thrown headlong into adventure, forced to go on the run under suspicion of murder. Fleeing to Scotland, with police and spies on his tail, he teams up with feisty suffragette Victoria Sinclair, and although they fail to hit it off, the couple become increasingly reliant on each other as they race to unmask a traitor and save Great Britain from German invasion. Action thriller, co-starring Eddie Marsan and Lydia Leonard

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Kristin, Breen and Gina witness a fatal skydiving accident while celebrating Mike's birthday with a picnic. To make matters worse, Kristin knows the victim - entrepreneur Andre Barrington, an ex-boyfriend. When the cords are found severed, it becomes clear that his demise was either suicide or murder

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The offer of a substantial reward inspires Hetty to find the bright spark behind a spate of arson attacks, and she begins by investigating the activities of a neighbour's foster daughter whose absences from home coincide with the fires. Housewife-detective series, starring Patricia Routledge, with Derek Benfield and Dominic Monaghan

programme.title: Rillington Place
programme.desc: After the strain of Timothy Evans' trial, Christie finds it increasingly difficult to maintain the charade. As his marriage breaks down he embarks upon a course of action that finally reveals his true nature to the world. Fact-based drama, starring Tim Roth, Samantha Morton, Nico Mirallegro and Jodie Comer

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Nikki travels to Mexico to attend the funeral of Luisa, a former intern at the Lyell Centre who was murdered in a carjacking in her native country. She soon stumbles across the post-mortem report and realises that the body is not that of her ex-colleague. Assisted by Dr Eva Vasquez and fired up with the idea that Luisa might still be alive, Nikki and Jack trace her last-known movements and are led to a farm where a cartel massacre was carried out. Crime drama, starring Emilia Fox

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Suzie drops a bombshell by revealing she's applied for a transfer and Sun Hill is left in shock when a rape victim visits the station and identifies Tony Stamp as her assailant. programme.title: Emma
programme.desc: Bafta award-winning writer Sandy Welch's dramatisation of Jane Austen's novel, first published in 1815. Romola Garai stars as the independent Emma Woodhouse, who believes she is a skilled matchmaker and tries to pair up her friends and acquaintances. She takes Harriet Smith under her wing and encourages the woman to set her sights higher than her current suitor - against Mr Knightley's warnings about the dangers of meddling. With Jonny Lee Miller, Michael Gambon, Jodhi May and Tamsin Greig

programme.title: Emma
programme.desc: While continuing her efforts to set up Harriet and the vicar, Emma looks forward to meeting Frank Churchill at the Christmas party, but when he fails to show up she becomes the subject of Mr Elton's unwanted attention. Later, village gossip focuses on the arrival of Jane Fairfax and a piano she was given by a mystery admirer, leaving the matchmaker reluctant to believe Mr Knightley could have sent the gift. Drama, starring Romola Garai, Louise Dylan, Blake Ritson and Laura Pyper

programme.title: Emma
programme.desc: The heroine plans a ball with Frank, and wonders whether she has feelings for him. Despite having a wonderful time at the event, she still feels trapped, and with the matchmaking effort officially abandoned, becomes increasingly bored. However, her spirits are raised by Mr Knightley's proposal for a group outing to Box Hill, where he tells her he thinks Frank and Jane are in love. Drama, starring Romola Garai and Michael Gambon

programme.title: Emma
programme.desc: The outing to Box Hill ends in disaster when Emma insults Miss Bates. After being berated by Knightley, she realises her behaviour was shameful and tries to make amends, but Jane refuses to speak to her. When Frank's overbearing aunt dies, the Westons expect him to propose to Emma, but his subsequent actions start a chain of events that shocks the heroine, resulting in the realisation of a truth she previously dismissed. Romola Garai and Jonny Lee Miller star in Sandy Welch's dramatisation of Jane Austen's novel

Crime drama, starring Ken Stott

programme.desc: Carol and Alan go away for the night, so Bianca has a party which gets out of control. Ricky comes to Natalie's rescue, Pat comes to her senses and Robbie can't get a girlfriend

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: As the inquiry into Recall's death takes place, Shauna's mood swings become more extreme and Adam fears Ross's presence in the pub has something to do with the DI Roebuck business

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Cardale prepares for Andrew and Erica's wedding, but a health scare at the local old people's home tests everyone to the limit. Rural medical drama, starring Gary Mavers and Fiona Gillies

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: A supergrass tires of life in protective custody, causing a disturbance which requires the attention of Dr Paige and DI Cramer. The informant's so-called safe house soon belies its name as a hail of bullets confirms his worst fears. Drama, starring Nigel Havers and Jane Gurnett

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: The girls visit an antiques market held in an old warehouse, but somehow get locked in. After several hours and numerous failed attempts to attract attention, Dorien is in desperate need of the loo, while Sharon and Tracey contemplate cannibalism. Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph star

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: The staff at Bayview Retirement Village are sent into a panic when Diana and Tom head off to Brighton for the day in a borrowed Porshe without telling anyone. Comedy, starring Graham Crowden and Stephanie Cole

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Rene and the resistance attempt to smuggle the airmen back to England inside empty landmines, but Herr Flick and Von Smallhausen hide in the mines awaiting resistance members

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Billy dons a Robin Hood outfit, and promptly sets Nora's heart a-flutter - much to the frustration of Compo, who responds by getting a costume of his own. Rural comedy, starring Bill Owen, Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton, with Keith Clifford and Kathy Staff

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Horticultural lecturer Rosemary Boxer is hired by a wealthy but ill businessman to help cure a disease causing his trees to lose their bark. She gets to know Laura Thyme, who turns up after a friend is killed in a suspicious car accident, and when the pair find the lord of the manor unconscious beneath his bedroom window, they must decide whether depression led him to attempt suicide or if there is a more sinister explanation. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal, Pam Ferris, David Mallinson and Roger Alborough

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The amateur sleuths uncover the remains of a racehorse while engaged on horticultural work in the grounds of pop star Nev Connolly's supposedly haunted mansion. Before long, gruesome revelations are coming thick and fast as Nev is shot dead and more bones are discovered - this time belonging to a young woman. As they investigate further, Rosemary and Laura are plunged into a sinister mystery with roots deep in the past. Drama, guest starring Murray Head (Heartbeat) and Abigail Cruttenden (Benidorm)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: When a retrospective exhibition of murdered fashion designer Ritchie Levene's work opens, the team is prompted to re-examine the three-year-old crime. The detectives learn about his tempestuous relationships with his new spouse, personal assistant and ex-wife Sarah, who believes his brother Adrian is the killer because he had the most to gain from his death. Meanwhile, Gerry enlists Emily to help update his image, although not everyone is impressed with the results. Crime drama, guest starring Rupert Graves and Hannah Waterman

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Quinn and his MI5 team search for a consignment of bombs lost between Ireland and the UK - and the subsequent death of a family planning expert makes them suspect dangerously unbalanced pro-life terrorist Mary Kane. Further investigations reveal she may have plans to detonate as many as 20 of the explosives as part of her campaign. Drama, starring Matthew Macfadyen, Keeley Hawes and Peter Firth

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Four bodies are found in the Thames after a boat full of illegal immigrants founders. Realising they are dealing with a people-trafficking operation gone disastrously wrong, Leo, Harry and Nikki set out to discover whether there are any survivors, while delving into the victims' pasts. Feature-length episode, guest starring Burt Kwouk

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Terry and Mickey investigate an armed robbery on a shop, and Matt Hinckley's arrival as CPS lawyer at Sun Hill prompts further heart-searching for Emma and Will. John Heaton zealously pursues his zero tolerance policy, while a hung-over Dale discovers Kezia doesn't remember their kiss from the night before. Angela Lonsdale guest stars

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover

The country's entry in the 1995 Rugby World Cup provides a perfect opportunity, and he enlists the aid of captain Francois Pienaar in making his team an inspiration to all South Africans. Clint Eastwood's fact-based drama, starring Morgan Freeman and Matt Damon

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Nikki Fox, Miles Jupp, Nish Kumar and Joe Lycett to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Z for Zachariah (2015)
programme.desc: A woman lives alone on a farm that belonged to her family in the aftermath of a nuclear apocalypse, believing herself to be the last survivor of the human race. Her simple life is disrupted by the appearance of a second survivor, and they adapt to working together - but the arrival of a third individual threatens everything they have built. Sci-fi drama based on Robert C O'Brien's novel, starring Margot Robbie, Chiwetel Ejiofor and Chris Pine

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A couple tell Martin Roberts the plans for their Stoke-on-Trent property and Martel Maxwell is in Leeds with a first-time developer. Dion Dublin views a Merseyside property

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts as experts Tim Weeks and Caroline Hawley help the reds and blue teams find bargains at the antiques centre in Elsecar, South Yorkshire. Plus, Anita views a Meissen collection

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum visits Cornwall's Eden Project, and JB Gill goes surfing with a Christian who came back to faith while catching the waves

programme.title: Live International T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents all the action from the second T20 in the three-match series, taking place at Ageas Bowl in Southampton. The Australians are the world's number one ranked side in this format, boasting enviable strength in depth with both the bat and ball. However, England prevailed by 28 runs at Edgbaston when the sides last met in a T20 prior to this series in 2018 with the help of strong bowling displays from Adil Rashid and Chris Jordan

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Close Shave
programme.desc: Nick Park's Oscar-winning animated characters embark on their third adventure. Wallace falls in love with the proprietor of the local wool shop - only for Gromit to be framed for sheep rustling. With the voices of Peter Sallis and Anne Reid

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani goes back to her Yorkshire roots exploring the lesser-known River Burn in the glorious Swinton estate. She discovers why people seem to have fallen in love with fishing since lockdown and how the humble willow is the perfect weapon against riverbank erosion. Meanwhile, Adam Henson meets this year's One Man and His Dog competitors from Northern Ireland and Wales, and John Craven joins Cerys Matthews and Simon King for the 2020 Photographic Competition

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce shares some of the best unseen items from recent Roadshows around the UK - from rare space memorabilia to a jewel-encrusted dagger, and a well-loved rocking horse that has entertained three generations of one family

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: Robin goes undercover once more to befriend a suspect and puts herself in a dangerous situation to find a vital piece of evidence, while Strike pieces together a theory by interviewing many of the key players in the case - but ends up fending off an attack in the process. Robin and Strike regroup and the person blackmailing of the government minister becomes their prime suspect - but they now believe that there was an accomplice. Crime thriller, starring Tom Burke and Holliday Grainger

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the opening round of fixtures in the Super League, featuring Manchester United v Chelsea at Leigh Sports Village, Arsenal v Reading at Meadow Park, Tottenham Hotspur v West Ham United at The Hive, Bristol City v Everton at Twerton Park and Brighton & Hove Albion v Birmingham City at The People's Pension Stadium

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: Follow-up series to Britain's Youngest Football Boss, focusing on West Ham United's women's team and managing director Jack Sullivan, son of Hammers co-chairman David. In the first episode, veteran midfielder Kate Longhurst is struggling to find a permanent place to live, and new signing Jacynta Galabadaarachchi is learning to adapt to life in Romford, away from her home in Australia. Narrated by Jaime Winstone

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker asks the questions in the light-hearted quiz, with celebrity guests AP McCoy, Ugo Monye, Jade Jones and Gary Pallister joining team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley visits a fake motorway used for training by emergency services in Gloucestershire, while Rav Wilding has the latest crime appeals from all over the UK

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a property in Dover, Martin Roberts looks around a house in the London suburb of Wood Green, and Martel Maxwell visits a project in Cumbria

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police try to recover what they suspect are stolen vehicles. Plus, how seized assets are sold off when a ring and a very prestigious Bentley are put up for auction

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright investigates the case of a vulnerable tenant trapped in a collapsing property, and meets a resident who has had enough of the abandoned house next door

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram assist the red and blue teams as they look for items of interest and value in Detling, Kent. Presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp monitors proceeding and also visits a nearby farm to lend a hand with the hop harvest

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Drama created by Roland Moore in 2009 to commemorate the 70th anniversary of the outbreak of the Second World War. As Britain struggles to contain the Nazi war machine, four females from different walks of life join the Women's Land Army, little realising their decision could change their futures. In the first episode, Nancy, who was forced to enlist, tries to get closer to Lord Hoxley at a dance. Starring Christine Bottomley, Summer Strallen, Becci Gemmell and Jo Woodcock, with Nathaniel Parker and Mark Benton

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers examine the history behind some of the nation's most famous dishes, beginning by exploring how the monarchy has influenced British cuisine. The duo also prepare a classic coronation chicken, and a beef dish named after Prince Albert, and food historian Ivan Day whips up a meal using a cookbook from the time of James II

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman meets an animal-loving house-hunter from Oxfordshire, who is looking for a home in Dorset - ideally close to the coast, and has a generous £700,000 budget

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich meet single mum Louise, who is hoping for somewhere to grow food and her two children to indulge in their hobbies of chess and gymnastics

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring three treasured family heirlooms back to life, restoring a set of grocer's scales, a leather keepsake bag and an ornamental Egyptian table

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Gethin Jones

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For any parent, making sure a child gets the best schooling is often a worry, but when your child has complex special educational needs or disabilities, it can cause real anxiety. As most children in England return to their classrooms, reporter Sean Dilley investigates the system for supporting young people with special educational needs. He meets families who, during lockdown, struggled without any support at all, and now, as their children head back to school, fear they may not get the right support to help them learn and stay safe in their classrooms. Sean discovers an adversarial system that was supposed to put the needs of children at its heart, but instead has created what some call 'a treacle of bureaucracy' for parents to navigate

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A luxury hotel chain makes penniless and exhausted couple Mike and Alison an offer they can't refuse, but the prospect of their departure drives a wedge between the ghosts, and the hardline Captain finds himself ostracised, while Pat is voted in as reluctant leader. As the clueless spirits brainstorm money-making schemes, the Captain takes matters into his own hands. Meanwhile, fortune smiles on Headless Humphrey in his ceaseless search for his errant body

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part one of two. The comedian travels along one of the world's most contentious borders, visiting places in America and Mexico to find out what life is like on both sides. Sue starts her journey at the western end of the border in the Mexican city of Tijuana, where she helps Honduran volunteers building a hostel for fellow refugees fleeing violence in their country

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: Detective Moses Mountree returns to Midsomer, an African warlord faces a new threat, and crooner Thaddeus Price sings a romantic song to his girlfriend

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: The three remaining queens write, record and perform an all-new remix of a RuPaul classic as they battle for the title of Canada's first Drag Race superstar

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Comedian and actress Jo Brand takes over the reins for an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Independent Group MP Heidi Allen and stand-up Phil Wang joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Lauren Layfield and Mark Wright build an 'eco den' for 11-year-old Whitney, an avid recycler who takes a bin bag everywhere she goes so she can deal with any litter she finds

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: A strange sea creature that likes to spew out its innards, the mysterious green balls invading an Australian beach and a peculiar pulsating blob found hiding in the city sewers

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: The most unusual places in the UK

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The team reveal the winner of the epic Supercar of the Future competition, look back at what viewers have been up to over summer and have a big surprise in store

programme.title: Deadly 60 on a Mission
programme.desc: The crew reaches the remote island of South Georgia, where Steve Backshall witnesses two elephant seals in battle as he walks with the largest colony of king penguins in the world. He's then given the opportunity to swim with the birds as they head offshore to feed, before travelling to his final destination - Antarctica. The naturalist is here with one mission - to dive with a leopard seal, one of the region's greatest predators

programme.title: Rise of the Continents
programme.desc: Iain Stewart examines clues hidden in the landscape to reveal how the Americas were formed from an ancient supercontinent, later named Pangaea. He finds evidence that 300 million years ago New York was at the heart of a huge mountain range, and travels to an extinct volcano in Bolivia to explore subduction - the process that has shaped South America

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The extraordinary abilities of the squirrel family are examined, putting their problem-solving to the test on a specially designed assault course. Some of the world's leading squirrel scientists reveal their groundbreaking discoveries - from the fox squirrel who can remember the location of 9,000 nuts, to the grey squirrel whose tree-top leaps are the basis of new designs in robotics

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: As autumn sets in, the farmers prepare for mating season. In Aberdeenshire, deer farmers Isla and George wrangle their herd of semi-wild deer closer to the farm to tackle the challenges of rutting season. In the Lake District, Matt and Murray invest in some new rams, while in Armadale, Joyce Campbell's 600 ewes get stuck in the mud while being herded to the hills. On the Stoer Peninsula, Helen and Graham take their small boat out during settled weather to collect their creel pots

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner joins choreographer Arlene Phillips as she remembers her childhood, and her determination to succeed as a dancer despite a difficult time at school and a great personal loss. He also finds out about the typical food she grew up with, producing two dishes that bring a taste of nostalgia while also paying tribute to her Jewish roots

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing's Len Goodman takes naturalist Steve Backshall to the wilds of Dorset to recreate an adventure camping holiday he and his family enjoyed back in 1982

programme.title: Escape to Athena (1979)
programme.desc: Second World War Allied PoWs incarcerated on a Greek island plan to defy the SS by breaking out of prison and pulling off an art robbery, with the help of their liberal-minded camp commandant. Action adventure, with Roger Moore, David Niven, Telly Savalas, Elliott Gould, Stefanie Powers and Claudia Cardinale

programme.title: Dancing Through the Blitz: Blackpool's Big Band Story
programme.desc: Jools Holland, Lucy Worsley and Len Goodman celebrate the big band sound and explore the social history behind the music that saw Britain through the Second World War in a programme originally made to commemorate the 70th anniversary of VE day. They trace the evolution of big band music from its beginnings in 1920s America to its glory days in the three decades that followed. Jools demonstrates how the big band sound developed through the influence of legends such as Duke Ellington, while Len and Lucy explore the dancing that accompanied big bands during the war, like the energetic Jitterbug. Featuring contributions by singer Petula Clark, comedian Roy Hudd, social historian Juliet Gardiner, and John Miller, nephew of bandleader Glenn Miller. Plus, extracts from a performance by Jools Holland and his Rhythm & Blues Orchestra at the Empress Ballroom in Blackpool

programme.title: Animals Behaving Badly
programme.desc: In the final episode, Liz Bonnin meets the animals going to extreme measures to survive, and reveals the some of the surprising scientific discoveries behind their ingenious behaviour. Liz examines the behaviour of cockatoos vandalising houses in Sydney, chimpanzees using bullying tactics to get to the top, crabs who hold anemones hostage to protect themselves, and the stone martens who cause millions of pounds worth of damage to cars while protecting their territories

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andi Oliver, Ivo Graham, Phill Jupitus and Kate Humble go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels by train through Austria and the Czech Republic, beginning in the northern Austrian city of Linz, where Adolf Hitler lived as a young boy. From here, Michael travels onto Prague Central Station, where he meets 87-year-old Zuzana Maresova, who, as a seven-year-old girl climbed aboard a train to travel to safety in London with hundreds of other Jewish children. Eventually crossing the Czech border into Slovakia, Michael reaches his final destination, one of Europe's youngest capital cities, Bratislava

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Captain Mainwaring initiates a recruitment campaign to attract new blood to Walmington-on-Sea's Home Guard - but makes the mistake of leaving Wilson in charge of the poster. Classic comedy first shown in 1975, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Royal History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian examines how writers, politicians and artists have manipulated the history of the Spanish Armada. She takes a look at the most famous parts of the story, from Francis Drake's game of bowls to Elizabeth I rousing her troops at Tilbury, and reveals how in Victorian times, the story was used as propaganda for the British Empire

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts the high-brow quiz, asking questions about ruffs, red items and other things beginning with the letter R. Trying to come up with the answers are Alan Davies, Tom Allen, Susan Calman and Zoe Lyons

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: First in a two-part pilot for the sci-fi drama. The 12 colonies of man are destroyed by robots, leaving just a few survivors to seek refuge aboard a starship. Guided by President Roslin and Commander Adama, they band together and plan their retaliation, hoping eventually they will stumble across the mythical 13th colony of mankind - Earth. Starring Mary McDonnell and Edward James Olmos

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Concluding part of the pilot for the sci-fi mini-series based on the cult Seventies drama. As the race to outwit the evil Cylons continues, it becomes clear there is a traitor on board - is there any hope of rediscovering the lost planet Earth? Edward James Olmos stars, alongside Mary McDonnell, Katee Sackhoff and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Ginger & Rosa (2012)
programme.desc: Two teenage girls living in 1960s London have been best friends all their lives - ever since their mothers met while giving birth in hospital - and often skip school to spend as much time as they can together. However, their relationship become strained as the Cuban missile crisis places the world on the brink of nuclear war. While one becomes involved in a protest movement, the other seeks meaning in her life through sexual experimentation. Drama, starring Elle Fanning, Alice Englert, Christina Hendricks, Annette Bening and Timothy Spall

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode continues his alphabetical tour through the BBC's culinary archive, this time selecting recipes and ingredients linked by the letter F. The Hairy Bikers cook a focaccia and Nigel Slater serves roast figs with honey and marsala

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In a special edition, food writer Mary Berry takes over as guest editor to reveal how farming and the countryside have influenced her life and career - and still do. Matt Baker is with her to discover what has inspired her, from early childhood on her parents' smallholding to the rural issues she holds dear today. Adam Henson investigates the state of British pig farming, while Anita Rani visits a school with a field-to-fork ethos, which is something Mary would love to see more of

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: A visit to Adam Frost's garden, catching up on his new patio project and finding out what his plans are for the autumn. Arit Anderson travels to Somerset to meet landscape designer Dan Pearson, to hear about the design and planting principles he has used to create his own garden. In London, film director and musician Don Letts and his wife reveal how they have combined their different tastes for plants and culture in their town garden. Plus, another chance to see Frances Tophill meet a gardener who grows most of the vegetable ingredients needed for Thai cooking in her back garden and the two enthusiasts with a passion for air plants at their nursery in Lancashire

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: George Anderson has taken a selection of cuttings, and now they have rooted he shows what to do next. Kirsty Wilson thinks of clever ways to display her house plants

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Odette (1950)
programme.desc: The French widow of an English soldier offers her services to British Intelligence, and is sent undercover as a spy in Nazi-occupied France. However, her work alongside the resistance leads to her falling into the clutches of the Gestapo. Fact-based Second World War drama, with Anna Neagle, Trevor Howard, Peter Ustinov and Marius Goring

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at television and film appearances made over the years by actress Deborah Kerr, capturing the milestones and highlights of her life and career that led to her being considered a Hollywood icon. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: Rev Kate Bottley and Ashley John-Baptiste visit projects to make the countryside more accessible, and discover how a town is coming together to tackle mental health issues

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades is at Walsall Recycling Centre, where he saves a set of lathe tools, a sofa and a collection of plant pots in the hope of breathing new life into them

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah is in the Warwickshire countryside helping a couple with a £750,000 budget find their ideal home, where an open-plan interior and a generous garden are priorities

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Steff Barker, who is obsessed with the idea of moving to New Zealand, and her husband Alan Goodwin, who finds even the idea of a move painful, see what Auckland has to offer

programme.title: Licence to Thrill - Paul Hollywood Meets Aston Martin
programme.desc: The Great British Bake-Off's Paul Hollywood hangs up his baker's apron to indulge his other passion, Aston Martin cars, which he has been obsessed with ever since he received a toy model of the Aston Martin DB5 driven by Sean Connery's Bond in Goldfinger. Paul is given a chance to test-drive the DBR1, which won the 1959 24 Hours of Le Mans championship, the DB5 he has cherished since childhood, and the DB10, which features as 007's new vehicle in the latest Bond film Spectre. The baker is also given an opportunity to join one of Aston's racing teams, and hears from the company's new boss Andy Palmer, who discusses his plans to improve the brand's financial fortunes

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: More highlights from the past two series of the motoring show, including Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness building their own electric sports cars for the inaugural 24 Minutes of Le Mansfield race. Plus, Chris also takes the electric Porsche Taycan for a spin and the show pays tribute to Colin McRae

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A colourful hair entrepreneur offers one Dragon a new look - and all of them a new investment opportunity. A cheesecake-maker has sweet dreams of a cash boost for his franchise model, but will his pitch be the stuff of nightmares? A husband-and-wife team hope their electrical invention will make sparks fly in the Den, as rival Dragons Deborah Meaden and Sara Davies line up to try it out. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer head to Norfolk, where they attempt to ensnare a large pike - a fish that has eluded them on their trips so far. As they arrive and get their gear together, Bob introduces Paul to the surprisingly varied contents of his car boot, much to Paul's amusement. They gather their equipment and find a decent spot that should give them plenty of chances to catch and make camp for the day. They end their trip with a visit to a local pub, where Bob reveals a hidden trick

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: The comedian looks back at the art of making a historical documentary. He examines a wide range of shows, including Kenneth Clarke's Civilisation, the works of AJP Taylor, James Burke and Mary Beard, all the way to Who Do You Think You Are and Danny Dyer's Right Royal Family, to discover what it takes to make a great TV history lesson

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker reflects on 25 years of his documentaries, featuring brand new conversations between Louis and some of his most memorable contributors. In this first edition, he looks back at his earliest documentaries, and explores how some people's most fervent beliefs can bring them into conflict with mainstream society. Louis catches up with some of his previous contributors including Mike Cain, who he first met in the mid-90s, having moved to the hills of Idaho for what he saw as an inevitable war with the federal government

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam and Phil pick up Frankie and Duke, who have been visiting their father. Frankie surprises Sam by saying she wants a big party for her 15th birthday

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam gets some bad news from the garage about a vibration problem with her car. Tessa rings with some good news - a revival of a popular kids' TV show may help with the bills both expected and unexpected

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Niki is forced to make a difficult decision to protect her family, while Matt and Audrey step up their hunt for Sylar - not realising he has already been captured by Mr Bennet. Peter is held in police custody after being mistakenly arrested in Odessa and Hiro races to save Claire. The meaning behind Isaac's painting of a nuclear bombing becomes clearer - with major consequences for one hero

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Peter awakens from his coma following a disturbing vision, and his journey to uncover the truth leads him to meet a new hero. Claire struggles to make sense of her life and Niki faces the consequences of her decision to turn herself in, while Hiro searches for the mysterious sword that will restore his power. Christopher Eccleston guest stars

programme.title: The Diagnosis Detectives
programme.desc: Michael Mosley challenges 12 of the UK's leading medical experts to make a diagnosis for patients whose debilitating symptoms have baffled other doctors. Paul's dreams of a happy retirement have been ruined by a mystery condition that has caused his face to swell up so dramatically that he sometimes struggles to see, while Judy struggles to eat solid food and is dramatically losing weight

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: When his father misses an appointment with his mental health worker, 25-year-old Oli calls Gloucestershire Police, concerned about the whereabouts of his father, Michael, who suffers from paranoid schizophrenia and is thought to have stopped taking his medication. With no sign of Michael overnight, police launch an appeal for information, and CCTV searches of the town reveal that Michael got into a taxi two nights before and was taken to a high point in the Cotswold's. Officers prepare for a major search operation, but looking for someone hiding in a heavily wooded area presents them with major challenges

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: It is the tiger cubs' first birthday bash, and Ben Fogle and Kate Humble look for signs that Longleat Safari Park's Southern Koala breeding programme - the only one in Europe - has resulted in a joey. Meanwhile, Jean Johansson joins keeper James to take a closer look at the anatomy of Gwen - a huge hairy tarantula

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich try to breathe life into a concrete courtyard in Greater Manchester. Nick and Dinah want cottage plants, vegetable beds and room for family meals

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's tour reaches its final stop, Singapore, where he learns about the island city's maritime origins and the vision of the 19th-century Englishman who put it on the map. At the bar of the Raffles Hotel, he treats himself to a Singapore Sling, now a world-famous cocktail, takes a boat down the river and dines with an artist and his family in Chinatown

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A selection of television interviews with actor David Niven, examining the appearances on chat shows he made over the years. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: A Matter of Life and Death (1946)
programme.desc: A Second World War bomber pilot survives jumping out of his stricken plane without a parachute and falls in love with the radio operator he had first spoken to during the fateful flight. A heavenly messenger is sent to tell him he should have died and that his presence is required in the afterlife, but he remains determined to continue his life on Earth and insists on a trial to resolve the issue. Powell and Pressburger's romantic fantasy, starring David Niven, Kim Hunter, Roger Livesey and Marius Goring

programme.title: Blitz Cities
programme.desc: Shane Richie takes an aerial journey over London to explore the effect of the Blitz on his home city, retracing the routes used by the Luftwaffe and talking to survivors

programme.title: Blitz Cities
programme.desc: Ricky Tomlinson takes an aerial journey over Liverpool to explore the effect the Blitz had on his home city from the perspective of those that dropped the bombs. He also hears from survivors on the ground, including his own brother, about how they coped in the city's greatest hour of need

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Philip Serrel and Catherine Southon find intricately carved walnuts and a silver epergne at Bletchley Park, Buckinghamshire, while Paul Martin meets Second World War codebreakers

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Dev Griffin, Alex Horne, Debbie McGee and June Sarpong test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Farmers and traditional horsemen Paul from Bedfordshire and John from Hampshire show the skill and passion needed to do succeed in one of the country's top ploughing competitions

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Poet Pam Ayres and her friend and actor Geoffrey Whitehead join experts James Braxton and Kate Bliss on a quest to find items to sell at an auction in Cirencester, Gloucestershire. Their search takes them through Hampshire and Berkshire, with Pam learning about the heroic exploits of the Glider Pilot Regiment and Geoffrey stopping in Southampton for a game on the world's oldest bowling green

programme.title: Tom Kerridge: Lose Weight for Good
programme.desc: This time Tom Kerridge tackles special treats, offering ideas for dishes that will hit the spot with some of the serious food-lovers and restaurant-goers among his group of dieters. The calorie-counted recipes he has created include a one-pot Mediterranean pasta dish, a rich and creamy beef stroganoff, and coffee and chocolate custard pots

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The eighth first-round match sees four students from the University of York pit their wits against a team from King's College London in a bid to reach the second stage. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: The Diagnosis Detectives
programme.desc: Michael Mosley challenges 12 of the UK's leading medical experts to treat two more patients living with debilitating symptoms that have baffled other doctors for years. The first patient is 64-year-old army veteran Neil, whose body is covered in a painful rash that itches so intensely he has scratched the top layer of his skin off. The second case is 35-year-old nursing assistant Katie, who has suffered a dramatic decline in health in the last 18 months and has lost four-and-a-half stone

programme.title: Inside Chelsea: Britain's Wealthiest Borough
programme.desc: Mehreen Baig investigates the Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea, an area that boasts the UK's most expensive houses and where annual salaries are three times the national average. Her guide is a true Chelsea insider - Amanda Eliasch, who made much of her fortune in a divorce from her billionaire husband 14 years ago. She's also a successful photographer, and achieved notoriety in 2019 when she hired an auction house to dispose of the items in her wardrobe - including 250 pairs of designer shoes

programme.title: The Archiveologists
programme.desc: Comedy performers Diane Morgan and Joe Wilkinson delve into the archive to parody a 1970s film on what to do at a business meeting

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Mark Urban

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker reflects on 25 years of his documentaries, featuring brand new conversations between Louis and some of his most memorable contributors. In this first edition, he looks back at his earliest documentaries, and explores how some people's most fervent beliefs can bring them into conflict with mainstream society. Louis catches up with some of his previous contributors including Mike Cain, who he first met in the mid-90s, having moved to the hills of Idaho for what he saw as an inevitable war with the federal government

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In a special edition, food writer Mary Berry takes over as guest editor to reveal how farming and the countryside have influenced her life and career - and still do. Matt Baker is with her to discover what has inspired her, from early childhood on her parents' smallholding to the rural issues she holds dear today. Adam Henson investigates the state of British pig farming, while Anita Rani visits a school with a field-to-fork ethos, which is something Mary would love to see more of

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Artists create pieces for buyers in Chesterfield who want something special to remember their beloved fathers and a stepdad, who they lost within the space of two years

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein re-examine the murder of a reclusive pensioner in Scotland in 1952, for which a farm labourer was hanged, despite claiming to have an alibi

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Summer arrives, and in the east of Scotland, Martin prepares two young bulls for the biggest event of the farming calendar - the Royal Highland Show in Edinburgh. First they need their nose rings, an accessory no bull can do without. Martin and Mel's wedding is also looming, and while he goes for his final kilt fitting, Mel meets her Dad to discuss their plans

programme.title: Pole to Pole
programme.desc: Michael Palin makes a hasty departure from Russia, travelling through Istanbul to Sudan on the third leg of his epic journey around the globe. Along the way he visits a British garrison in Cyprus, works up a sweat in a Turkish bath and mingles with the 3,000 guests at a Greek Orthodox wedding

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Saga and Martin continue to hunt down the eco-terrorists, who realise they are being followed by the police and proceed with caution. Meanwhile, Jakob realises that living with his girlfriend is not always easy, and Martin makes progress with Jens. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Saga and Martin set out to track down the leader of the eco-terrorists, hoping to stop any further attacks being planned. Meanwhile, Martin hits it off with his Danish colleague Pernille. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: The investigation grows, offering up new leads. Meanwhile, four people wearing animal masks carry out a new attack, before posting footage of the assault online. Saga and Martin try to track down the perpetrators before they cause more harm, but they are already busy planning their next move. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Saga visits Martin and his ailing son at the hospital and notices something is not quite right about the place. Meanwhile, the investigation intensifies as another poisoning is connected to the duo's case, and a motorboat containing skeletal remains is recovered from the bottom of the sea. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1977
programme.desc: A selection of outstanding complete archive performances from Britain's Silver Jubilee year. It was a period dominated by funk and punk with Heatwave's Boogie Nights and te Stranglers' No More Heroes in the top 10. Classic top of the charts hits included Baccara with Yes Sir, I Can Boogie and Angelo by Brotherhood of Man. Some of the enduring heroes to take to the stage that year included David Bowie, Rod Stewart, Queen and Elvis Costello, and this showcase also features a rare studio performances from Bob Marley and the Wailers, and the Jacksons

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Summer arrives, and in the east of Scotland, Martin prepares two young bulls for the biggest event of the farming calendar - the Royal Highland Show in Edinburgh. First they need their nose rings, an accessory no bull can do without. Martin and Mel's wedding is also looming, and while he goes for his final kilt fitting, Mel meets her Dad to discuss their plans

programme.title: Lost Home Movies of Nazi Germany
programme.desc: Part two of two. Amateur footage from the latter half of the Second World War, offering an insight into life and atrocities in Germany and the occupied territories as Hitler's dreams crumble and the moral reckoning the German people had to face. The films reveal the stories of people battling to save their families from deportation to the death camps, while others endure the horrors of ever more deadly bombing raids, all set against a backdrop of propaganda and false hope pouring forth from Nazi high command

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James charts the history of 4x4 vehicles from the jeeps of the Second World war onwards, revealing how they rose in popularity in everyday use and in rallies. He explores the rivalry between Land Rover and Land Cruiser, and puts some classic vehicles through their paces by driving up Mount Fuji and through the Mojave desert

programme.title: BBC Proms 2020
programme.desc: Mercury Prize-nominated artist Laura Marling teams up with the pioneering strings-based collective the 12 Ensemble for a retrospective journey through her back catalogue, as well as showcasing tracks from her current album, Song for our Daughter. In her first Prom as a headliner, Laura will perform an acoustic set accompanied by brand-new string arrangements from the unconducted 12 Ensemble. Presented by Suzy Klein

programme.title: The Flying Archaeologist
programme.desc: Ben Robinson flies over Wiltshire to uncover new discoveries in the Stone Age landscape of Stonehenge. Sites found from the air have led to exciting evidence that helps to explain why the monument's location was chosen and reveals at what point in history it was inhabited

programme.title: Opera Mums with Bryony Kimmings
programme.desc: Inspired by the lives of single mums and informed by opera experts at the English National Opera, performance artist Bryony Kimmings writes and directs her own operetta. The demands of the opera industry are turned on their head by Kimmings' insistence that real life is just as deserving of high art as the stories of the rich and powerful

programme.title: Danielle de Niese: The Birth of an Opera
programme.desc: The internationally acclaimed soprano provides a backstage pass to her preparations for the role of Rosina in Glyndebourne's 2016 production of Rossini's The Barber of Seville. Masterclass sessions with director Annabel Arden, conductor Enrique Mazzola and other key cast members explore key scenes in depth, and Danni visits the Rome theatre where the disastrous premiere took place in 1816. Plus, extensive footage from the staged version of the opera

programme.title: America's Greatest Opera House: The Story of the Met
programme.desc: The creation of the Metropolitan Opera's home of the last 50 years, focusing on the artists, architects and politicians who shaped the cultural life of New York in the '50s and 60s. Among the notable figures in the film are famed soprano Leontyne Price, who opened the new Met in 1966 in Samuel Barber's Antony and Cleopatra, and Rudolf Bing, the Met's general manager, who engineered the move from the old house to the new one

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Dawn's worst fears become a reality when Jase puts Jay before her and Summer and May reappears on the Square. Heather messes up the food order at the Vic

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Dawn is frantic with worry knowing May has returned, Abi disappears after feigning illness and arguing with Tanya, and Ian catches Lucy in a compromising position

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: The residents of the Square rally round to help Dawn, Mickey and Summer, who are trapped in the burning house, but it's Keith who shocks everyone with his heroic actions

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Immersive viewing experience, featuring a combination of sequences from natural history programmes with narration from Buddhist monk Andy Puddicombe

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene featuring a mountain against the backdrop of a clear, blue sky

programme.title: Africa with Ade Adepitan
programme.desc: Ade concludes his journey by exploring southern Africa. In Mozambique, he discovers that a declining tourist trade has benefited wildlife and explores local attitudes to disability. Moving on to South Africa, he investigates how much life has changed in the 25 years since the downfall of apartheid. His journey ends in Zimbabwe, a country that is still struggling economically despite having vast quantities of gold

programme.title: Africa Turns the Page: The Novels That Shaped a Continent
programme.desc: Nigerian-born presenter and historian David Olusoga explores the story of the African novel, and a time when writers including Chinua Achebe, Ngugi wa Thiong'o and Wole Soyinka became the conscience of a continent. In their wake, the African novel was to spread around the world - writers of the African diaspora such as Buchi Emecheta and Ben Okri created masterpieces from their adopted home of the United Kingdom. Featuring contributions by Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie, Aminatta Forna and 2019 Booker winner Bernardine Evaristo

programme.title: Mexico: Earth's Festival of Life
programme.desc: The first of three programmes about the wildlife, landscape and culture in different areas of the country explores the Sierra Madre mountains, and reveals how this rugged landscape has shaped the nation. This chain of peaks includes several volcanoes and is home to a wide variety of animals, including black bears, monarch butterflies, orchid bees and quetzals. The programme also visits the ruins of an ancient Aztec shrine and the blue agave plant which grows in the mountains is used in making tequila

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary using archive performance footage and interviews to explore the life of Miriam Makeba, the Johannesburg-born singer and civil rights activist who was forced into exile in 1960 for opposing the apartheid regime. Miriam became one of the first South African musicians to make a name for herself on the international scene, and she used her music to deliver messages against racism and poverty

programme.title: Art on the BBC
programme.desc: Michelangelo was not merely a giant of the Renaissance. He was also one of the era's most controversial personalities. Art historian Sona Datta explores six decades of BBC archive to discover how TV has influenced our understanding of him. Sona reveals how TV has tried to reconcile Michelangelo's art with his difficult personality, bringing to life the story of a man who rose from humble beginnings to favoured artist of the rich and powerful

programme.title: Jigs & Wigs
programme.desc: Tyrone native and Irish dance fitness coach Angela Mohan puts her army of athletes through the drill in the lead up to the World Championships. Documentary series in which cameras follow dancing advocates in an attempt to illustrate the vibrant and progressive world they inhabit

programme.desc: The lives of two young brothers living on the Island of Fofoa in Tonga.
