programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Outside the clubhouse a large crowd gathers around a hot-air balloon, with whisperings that someone is going on an adventure

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Jemima is short on fruit ingredients for her flapjacks, and in a misunderstanding with Peter, she heads to Mr McGregor's garden to find some

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day travels back 30,000 years to the last ice age, where he is stalked by a cave lion and joins woolly mammoths on their annual migration

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog Loves Art Pug wants to learn how to paint, so our heroes visit Picasso's studio in Paris.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle is staying at Angela's kennels while the Brooklyn-Bells are on holiday.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran are having a spring clear out, but when Gran Gran wants to clear out her old jumper, JoJo is distraught - she loves that snuggly jumper!

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Tig is given a bicycle for her birthday, but will not take advice from her mother on how to ride it

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Pepi Nana finds a plate of toy carrots on the table in the Toy House

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Major Clanger is making too much noise in his workshop and Mother is fed up with it. She suggests they should swap jobs

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy dances in the garden with the Haahoos and the daisies

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Pete Dalton reads Itchy Itch Itch, by Mara Bergman

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Host Matt Tebbutt is joined by podcast-presenting couple Chris and Rosie Ramsey and chef Yotam Ottolenghi. Plus, clips of Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain and Sam Evans

programme.title: Nigellissima
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson treats her supper guests to a feast of lamb chops with mint, chilli and potatoes, served alongside roasted red onions with fennel and basil. Dessert is a true Anglo-Italian affair with a plum and amaretti crumble. There are also recipes for farro risotto with mushrooms, and chocolate pasta with a dark butterscotch sauce

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts is by the seaside in Norfolk, Martel Maxwell encounters a house with no stairs in Lancashire and Dion Dublin is in Staffordshire at a home with a carpeted bath

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents live coverage of day two of the annual event, which this year takes place at Manchester Regional Arena. Holly Bradshaw is aiming to win her eighth national pole vault title and local favourite Naomi Ogbeta will hope to make an impact in the triple jump, while the men's and women's 200m finals take place on the track. With commentary from Steve Cram and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Alice Through the Looking Glass (2016)
programme.desc: Alice Kingsleigh has spent three years sailing the high seas. Upon her return to London from China, she returns to the magical world of Underland and takes a journey back in time to save the Mad Hatter and her friends. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Mia Wasikowska, Johnny Depp and Anne Hathaway

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic clips from the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this edition, the contestants include a psychiatrist from London, a drama student from Leicester, and a bus driver from Derbyshire

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a couples special of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants including Harry Judd, Izzy Judd, Ade Adepitan, Elle Exxe, Dan Renton Skinner, Olivia Lee, Nik Speakman and Eva Speakman

programme.title: Invictus (2009)
programme.desc: Following his election as president of South Africa, Nelson Mandela finds himself the leader of a racially and economically divided nation, and seeks a means to unite the people. The country's entry in the 1995 Rugby World Cup provides a perfect opportunity, and he enlists the aid of captain Francois Pienaar in making his team an inspiration to all South Africans. Clint Eastwood's fact-based drama, starring Morgan Freeman and Matt Damon

programme.title: Don't Take Me Home
programme.desc: A look at how Wales fared at Euro 2016, the first time they had qualified for a major international football tournament since the 1958 World Cup. Featuring match highlights and interviews with the players and coaching staff.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Nikki Fox, Miles Jupp, Nish Kumar and Joe Lycett to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes is disappointed to discover she is not invited to Maria's hen party because of her age, and decides to gatecrash the event, only to end up at the wrong venue. She then tries to impress the bride-to-be's mother during a dinner date, but is so nervous she turns to drink, and the evening descends into chaos. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A couple tell Martin Roberts the plans for their Stoke-on-Trent property and Martel Maxwell is in Leeds with a first-time developer. Dion Dublin views a Merseyside property

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts as experts Tim Weeks and Caroline Hawley help the reds and blue teams find bargains at the antiques centre in Elsecar, South Yorkshire. Plus, Anita views a Meissen collection

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum visits Cornwall's Eden Project, and JB Gill goes surfing with a Christian who came back to faith while catching the waves

programme.title: Live International T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents all the action from the second T20 in the three-match series, taking place at Ageas Bowl in Southampton. The Australians are the world's number one ranked side in this format, boasting enviable strength in depth with both the bat and ball. However, England prevailed by 28 runs at Edgbaston when the sides last met in a T20 prior to this series in 2018 with the help of strong bowling displays from Adil Rashid and Chris Jordan

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Close Shave
programme.desc: Nick Park's Oscar-winning animated characters embark on their third adventure. Wallace falls in love with the proprietor of the local wool shop - only for Gromit to be framed for sheep rustling. With the voices of Peter Sallis and Anne Reid

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani goes back to her Yorkshire roots exploring the lesser-known River Burn in the glorious Swinton estate. She discovers why people seem to have fallen in love with fishing since lockdown and how the humble willow is the perfect weapon against riverbank erosion. Meanwhile, Adam Henson meets this year's One Man and His Dog competitors from Northern Ireland and Wales, and John Craven joins Cerys Matthews and Simon King for the 2020 Photographic Competition

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce shares some of the best unseen items from recent Roadshows around the UK - from rare space memorabilia to a jewel-encrusted dagger, and a well-loved rocking horse that has entertained three generations of one family

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: Robin goes undercover once more to befriend a suspect and puts herself in a dangerous situation to find a vital piece of evidence, while Strike pieces together a theory by interviewing many of the key players in the case - but ends up fending off an attack in the process. Robin and Strike regroup and the person blackmailing of the government minister becomes their prime suspect - but they now believe that there was an accomplice. Crime thriller, starring Tom Burke and Holliday Grainger

programme.title: Match of the Day Wales
programme.desc: Wales v Bulgaria. Jason Mohammad is joined by Jess Fishlock to present action from the UEFA Nations League Group B4 encounter at Cardiff City Stadium

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the opening round of fixtures in the Super League, featuring Manchester United v Chelsea at Leigh Sports Village, Arsenal v Reading at Meadow Park, Tottenham Hotspur v West Ham United at The Hive, Bristol City v Everton at Twerton Park and Brighton & Hove Albion v Birmingham City at The People's Pension Stadium

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: Follow-up series to Britain's Youngest Football Boss, focusing on West Ham United's women's team and managing director Jack Sullivan, son of Hammers co-chairman David. In the first episode, veteran midfielder Kate Longhurst is struggling to find a permanent place to live, and new signing Jacynta Galabadaarachchi is learning to adapt to life in Romford, away from her home in Australia. Narrated by Jaime Winstone

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley visits a fake motorway used for training by emergency services in Gloucestershire, while Rav Wilding has the latest crime appeals from all over the UK

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a property in Dover, Martin Roberts looks around a house in the London suburb of Wood Green, and Martel Maxwell visits a project in Cumbria

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police try to recover what they suspect are stolen vehicles. Plus, how seized assets are sold off when a ring and a very prestigious Bentley are put up for auction

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright investigates the case of a vulnerable tenant trapped in a collapsing property, and meets a resident who has had enough of the abandoned house next door

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram assist the red and blue teams as they look for items of interest and value in Detling, Kent. Presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp monitors proceeding and also visits a nearby farm to lend a hand with the hop harvest

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Drama created by Roland Moore in 2009 to commemorate the 70th anniversary of the outbreak of the Second World War. As Britain struggles to contain the Nazi war machine, four females from different walks of life join the Women's Land Army, little realising their decision could change their futures. In the first episode, Nancy, who was forced to enlist, tries to get closer to Lord Hoxley at a dance. Starring Christine Bottomley, Summer Strallen, Becci Gemmell and Jo Woodcock, with Nathaniel Parker and Mark Benton

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers examine the history behind some of the nation's most famous dishes, beginning by exploring how the monarchy has influenced British cuisine. The duo also prepare a classic coronation chicken, and a beef dish named after Prince Albert, and food historian Ivan Day whips up a meal using a cookbook from the time of James II

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman meets an animal-loving house-hunter from Oxfordshire, who is looking for a home in Dorset - ideally close to the coast, and has a generous £700,000 budget

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich meet single mum Louise, who is hoping for somewhere to grow food and her two children to indulge in their hobbies of chess and gymnastics

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring three treasured family heirlooms back to life, restoring a set of grocer's scales, a leather keepsake bag and an ornamental Egyptian table

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Gethin Jones

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For any parent, making sure a child gets the best schooling is often a worry, but when your child has complex special educational needs or disabilities, it can cause real anxiety. As most children in England return to their classrooms, reporter Sean Dilley investigates the system for supporting young people with special educational needs. He meets families who, during lockdown, struggled without any support at all, and now, as their children head back to school, fear they may not get the right support to help them learn and stay safe in their classrooms. Sean discovers an adversarial system that was supposed to put the needs of children at its heart, but instead has created what some call 'a treacle of bureaucracy' for parents to navigate

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: The Crash Detectives
programme.desc: When a head-on crash leaves a grandfather dead, Chris discovers the answers could lie in electronic data within the car. As his mission to access it begins, he unearths an intriguing story of what really led up to the incident

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part one of two. The comedian travels along one of the world's most contentious borders, visiting places in America and Mexico to find out what life is like on both sides. Sue starts her journey at the western end of the border in the Mexican city of Tijuana, where she helps Honduran volunteers building a hostel for fellow refugees fleeing violence in their country

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: Detective Moses Mountree returns to Midsomer, an African warlord faces a new threat, and crooner Thaddeus Price sings a romantic song to his girlfriend

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: The three remaining queens write, record and perform an all-new remix of a RuPaul classic as they battle for the title of Canada's first Drag Race superstar

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Comedian and actress Jo Brand takes over the reins for an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Independent Group MP Heidi Allen and stand-up Phil Wang joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.desc: Host Matt Tebbutt is joined by podcast-presenting couple Chris and Rosie Ramsey and chef Yotam Ottolenghi. Plus, clips of Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain and Sam Evans

programme.title: Z for Zachariah (2015)
programme.desc: A woman lives alone on a farm that belonged to her family in the aftermath of a nuclear apocalypse, believing herself to be the last survivor of the human race. Her simple life is disrupted by the appearance of a second survivor, and they adapt to working together - but the arrival of a third individual threatens everything they have built. Sci-fi drama based on Robert C O'Brien's novel, starring Margot Robbie, Chiwetel Ejiofor and Chris Pine

In this edition, the contestants include a psychiatrist from London, a drama student from Leicester, and a bus driver from Derbyshire

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a couples special of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants including Harry Judd, Izzy Judd, Ade Adepitan, Elle Exxe, Dan Renton Skinner, Olivia Lee, Nik Speakman and Eva Speakman

programme.title: Invictus (2009)
programme.desc: Following his election as president of South Africa, Nelson Mandela finds himself the leader of a racially and economically divided nation, and seeks a means to unite the people. The country's entry in the 1995 Rugby World Cup provides a perfect opportunity, and he enlists the aid of captain Francois Pienaar in making his team an inspiration to all South Africans. Clint Eastwood's fact-based drama, starring Morgan Freeman and Matt Damon

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Nikki Fox, Miles Jupp, Nish Kumar and Joe Lycett to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves

programme.title: Z for Zachariah (2015)
programme.desc: A woman lives alone on a farm that belonged to her family in the aftermath of a nuclear apocalypse, believing herself to be the last survivor of the human race. Her simple life is disrupted by the appearance of a second survivor, and they adapt to working together - but the arrival of a third individual threatens everything they have built. Sci-fi drama based on Robert C O'Brien's novel, starring Margot Robbie, Chiwetel Ejiofor and Chris Pine

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A couple tell Martin Roberts the plans for their Stoke-on-Trent property and Martel Maxwell is in Leeds with a first-time developer. Dion Dublin views a Merseyside property

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts as experts Tim Weeks and Caroline Hawley help the reds and blue teams find bargains at the antiques centre in Elsecar, South Yorkshire. Plus, Anita views a Meissen collection

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum visits Cornwall's Eden Project, and JB Gill goes surfing with a Christian who came back to faith while catching the waves

programme.title: Live International T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents all the action from the second T20 in the three-match series, taking place at Ageas Bowl in Southampton. The Australians are the world's number one ranked side in this format, boasting enviable strength in depth with both the bat and ball. However, England prevailed by 28 runs at Edgbaston when the sides last met in a T20 prior to this series in 2018 with the help of strong bowling displays from Adil Rashid and Chris Jordan

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Close Shave
programme.desc: Nick Park's Oscar-winning animated characters embark on their third adventure. Wallace falls in love with the proprietor of the local wool shop - only for Gromit to be framed for sheep rustling. With the voices of Peter Sallis and Anne Reid

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani goes back to her Yorkshire roots exploring the lesser-known River Burn in the glorious Swinton estate. She discovers why people seem to have fallen in love with fishing since lockdown and how the humble willow is the perfect weapon against riverbank erosion. Meanwhile, Adam Henson meets this year's One Man and His Dog competitors from Northern Ireland and Wales, and John Craven joins Cerys Matthews and Simon King for the 2020 Photographic Competition

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce shares some of the best unseen items from recent Roadshows around the UK - from rare space memorabilia to a jewel-encrusted dagger, and a well-loved rocking horse that has entertained three generations of one family

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: Robin goes undercover once more to befriend a suspect and puts herself in a dangerous situation to find a vital piece of evidence, while Strike pieces together a theory by interviewing many of the key players in the case - but ends up fending off an attack in the process. Robin and Strike regroup and the person blackmailing of the government minister becomes their prime suspect - but they now believe that there was an accomplice. Crime thriller, starring Tom Burke and Holliday Grainger

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the opening round of fixtures in the Super League, featuring Manchester United v Chelsea at Leigh Sports Village, Arsenal v Reading at Meadow Park, Tottenham Hotspur v West Ham United at The Hive, Bristol City v Everton at Twerton Park and Brighton & Hove Albion v Birmingham City at The People's Pension Stadium

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: Follow-up series to Britain's Youngest Football Boss, focusing on West Ham United's women's team and managing director Jack Sullivan, son of Hammers co-chairman David. In the first episode, veteran midfielder Kate Longhurst is struggling to find a permanent place to live, and new signing Jacynta Galabadaarachchi is learning to adapt to life in Romford, away from her home in Australia. Narrated by Jaime Winstone

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker asks the questions in the light-hearted quiz, with celebrity guests AP McCoy, Ugo Monye, Jade Jones and Gary Pallister joining team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley visits a fake motorway used for training by emergency services in Gloucestershire, while Rav Wilding has the latest crime appeals from all over the UK

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a property in Dover, Martin Roberts looks around a house in the London suburb of Wood Green, and Martel Maxwell visits a project in Cumbria

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police try to recover what they suspect are stolen vehicles. Plus, how seized assets are sold off when a ring and a very prestigious Bentley are put up for auction

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright investigates the case of a vulnerable tenant trapped in a collapsing property, and meets a resident who has had enough of the abandoned house next door

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram assist the red and blue teams as they look for items of interest and value in Detling, Kent. Presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp monitors proceeding and also visits a nearby farm to lend a hand with the hop harvest

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Drama created by Roland Moore in 2009 to commemorate the 70th anniversary of the outbreak of the Second World War. As Britain struggles to contain the Nazi war machine, four females from different walks of life join the Women's Land Army, little realising their decision could change their futures. In the first episode, Nancy, who was forced to enlist, tries to get closer to Lord Hoxley at a dance. Starring Christine Bottomley, Summer Strallen, Becci Gemmell and Jo Woodcock, with Nathaniel Parker and Mark Benton

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers examine the history behind some of the nation's most famous dishes, beginning by exploring how the monarchy has influenced British cuisine. The duo also prepare a classic coronation chicken, and a beef dish named after Prince Albert, and food historian Ivan Day whips up a meal using a cookbook from the time of James II

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman meets an animal-loving house-hunter from Oxfordshire, who is looking for a home in Dorset - ideally close to the coast, and has a generous £700,000 budget

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich meet single mum Louise, who is hoping for somewhere to grow food and her two children to indulge in their hobbies of chess and gymnastics

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring three treasured family heirlooms back to life, restoring a set of grocer's scales, a leather keepsake bag and an ornamental Egyptian table

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Gethin Jones

programme.title: Home Ground
programme.desc: The return of the rural affairs magazine, with Gavin Andrews helping with the vegetable harvest while Jo Scott meets women using social media to highlight their contribution to farming

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For any parent, making sure a child gets the best schooling is often a worry, but when your child has complex special educational needs or disabilities, it can cause real anxiety. As most children in England return to their classrooms, reporter Sean Dilley investigates the system for supporting young people with special educational needs. He meets families who, during lockdown, struggled without any support at all, and now, as their children head back to school, fear they may not get the right support to help them learn and stay safe in their classrooms. Sean discovers an adversarial system that was supposed to put the needs of children at its heart, but instead has created what some call 'a treacle of bureaucracy' for parents to navigate

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part one of two. The comedian travels along one of the world's most contentious borders, visiting places in America and Mexico to find out what life is like on both sides. Sue starts her journey at the western end of the border in the Mexican city of Tijuana, where she helps Honduran volunteers building a hostel for fellow refugees fleeing violence in their country

programme.title: Match of the Day from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Northern Ireland v Norway. Stephen Watson presents action from the Group B1 encounter at Windsor Park. The hosts prevailed 2-0 when the sides last met here in 2017 and Ian Baraclough will have been hoping to see another victory in his first home game as manager. With analysis by Tommy Wright

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: Detective Moses Mountree returns to Midsomer, an African warlord faces a new threat, and crooner Thaddeus Price sings a romantic song to his girlfriend

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: The three remaining queens write, record and perform an all-new remix of a RuPaul classic as they battle for the title of Canada's first Drag Race superstar

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Comedian and actress Jo Brand takes over the reins for an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Independent Group MP Heidi Allen and stand-up Phil Wang joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Education Committee's session on the impact of COVID-19 on education and children's services with evidence from Roger Taylor, Chair, Ofqual, Dr Michelle Meadows, Executive Director for Strategy and Research, Ofqual, Julie Swan, Executive Director, General Qualifications and Dame Glenys Stacey, Acting Chief Regulator, Ofqual, from Wednesday 2 September

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of a Foreign Affairs Committee session taking evidence on the UK's role in international organisations from a panel including former Australian Prime Minister Tony Abbott, from Tuesday 1 September

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: The statement in the House of Commons by the Education Secretary, Gavin Williamson, on the exam results problems over the summer and the reopening of schools and colleges.

programme.title: Scottish First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of questions to First Minister Nicola Sturgeon in the Scottish Parliament

programme.title: Scottish Parliament
programme.desc: The statement in the Scottish Parliament by the First Minister, Nicola Sturgeon, outlining the Scottish government's Programme for Government, from earlier today

programme.title: Scottish Parliament
programme.desc: Debate on Scottish Government's Programme for Government, from Wednesday 2 September

programme.title: Scottish Parliament
programme.desc: Scottish Parliament Committee's inquiry into the development of the Scottish Government policy on handling harassment complaints involving current and former Ministers with evidence from Dave Penman, General Secretary, FDA, Malcolm Clark, Convenor of the Council of Scottish Government Unions and PCS Scottish Government Group President, from Tuesday 1 September.

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Northern Ireland Affairs Committee's session on addressing the legacy of Northern Ireland's past: The UK Government's new proposals with evidence from Simon Byrne, Chief Constable, Police Service of Northern Ireland, Bobby Singleton, Detective Chief Superintendent, Police Service of Northern Ireland and Jon Boutcher, Head of Operations, Operation Kenova, from Wednesday 2 September.

programme.title: Ten Minute Rule Bill
programme.desc: Lib Dem MP Christine Jardine introduces her Immigration (Health and Social Care Staff) Bill, from earlier today

programme.title: Northern Ireland Questions
programme.desc: Questions in the House of Commons to the Northern Ireland Secretary Brandon Lewis and his team of ministers, from earlier today

programme.title: Ceitidh Morag (Katie Morag)

programme.title: Drop Dead Weird

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by guests Marvin Humes and Suzi Perry, and chefs Daniel Clifford and Bettina Campolucci Bordi. James serves up meatballs, soufflé, quiche and chicken chipotle, as well as providing a Masterclass in honeycomb

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott is joined by Alison Hammond and Aldo Zilli in the first episode of a new cookery series, which focuses on dishes that conjure up memories of holidays. Ainsley prepares spicy Seafood linguine with chilli and cherry tomatoes, Alison makes a Tunisian inspired lamb and cous cous dish, and Aldo gives a masterclass in making crispy chilli and parsley calamari. Finally, Ainsley also whips up a souvlaki and tiramisu

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef visits the region of Jura, where a local farmer shows him around his orchards and teaches him about the region's apples and pears, which James uses as the accompaniment in a barbecued duck. He also visits Fort des Rousses, the old military stronghold that is now used for the making of Comte, one of the country's most popular cheeses

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of this afternoon's meetings, including the 2.35 September Stakes from Kempton Park, the 4.05 Sprint Cup from Haydock Park and action from Ascot. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Adele Mulrennan, reports by Matt Chapman, Sally Ann Grassick and Oli Bell, and commentary from Mark Johnson

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Rolling in It
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a game show featuring a giant arcade machine, which three members of the public have to play to win thousands of pounds. In the final episode, Stephen is joined by guests Mark Wright, Fleur East and Samia Longchambon

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Perry Fenwick, Jenny Eclair, Ore Oduba and Richard Madeley pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the first semi-final of this year's talent contest, making a return to screens following the auditions episodes broadcast in the spring. Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, David Walliams and Ashley Banjo are on the judging desk, as the acts that impressed them the most return, including the golden buzzer acts - singer Fayth Ifil, comedian and singer Jon Courtney, choir Sign Along With Us, comedian Nabil Abdulrashid, and mother and daughter singing duo, Honey and Sammy. Eight acts perform in each semi-final, with two finalists selected by the judges and the votes of viewers

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Vinnie Jones joins Piers Morgan for an intimate Life Stories special, 10 years on from his first appearance on the show. Vinnie opens up about how his life has changed irrevocably in that time, as he comes to terms with grieving for the loss of his wife, Tanya, who passed away last year, after a six-year battle with cancer. He also looks back at some of the highlights of his football and movie careers, talks about his struggles with alcohol, how he is hoping to help other people, particularly men, to cope with grief and what the future now holds

programme.title: UEFA Nations League Highlights
programme.desc: Iceland v England. Mark Pougatch presents action from both teams' opening Group A2 encounter at Laugardalsvollur in Reykjavík. Plus, the key moments from Denmark v Belgium and Sweden v France. With analysis from Ian Wright and Roy Keane and reports by Gabriel Clarke, Richard Henwood and Celina Hinchcliffe, and commentary from Sam Matterface, Lee Dixon, Joe Speight and Seb Hutchinson

programme.title: James Nesbitt: A Game of Two Halves
programme.desc: The accomplished actor embarks on an epic journey to see how Soccer Aid for Unicef is helping children around the world. Since 2006, Soccer Aid has raised over £38million and James will see just how far that money goes. His mission is to take essential supplies to children living in three extreme locations - the Himalayas in Nepal, the desert surrounding the Dead Sea and a remote island in the South Pacific. For James however, the journey is one into the unknown, as he won't know where he is going on each leg of his mission until just before he leaves

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage eight of the most prestigious race on the calendar, which featured a mountainous 141km route from Cazeres-sur-Garonne to Loudenvielle. programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh presents the first in a series of special episodes in which he reveals the secrets behind some of his favourite garden designs, and looks back on some of the most memorable assignments featured on the show. The first episode focuses on country gardens, and revisits three past projects the team put inventive spins on - a classic country garden, a design with a cottage twist, and finally a project that set out to create an enchanted atmosphere inspired by classic children's book The Secret Garden

programme.title: Soccer Aid
programme.desc: England v Soccer Aid World XI (Kick-off TBA). Dermot O'Leary and Kirsty Gallacher present the pre-match build-up and full live coverage of the pro-celebrity charity football match to raise money in aid of international charity Unicef, which takes place this year at Old Trafford. The England side announced so far includes Joel Dommett, Marvin Humes, Danny Jones, Olly Murs, Joe Wicks and Mark Wright, backed up by legends Wes Brown, Katie Chapman, Andy Cole, David James and Kelly Smith. They will be managed by Sam Allardyce and Wayne Rooney. The World XI side includes Patrice Evra, Yaya Toure, Jason Manford, Iain Stirling, Chelcee Grimes and Kem Cetinay, and they will be hoping to lift the trophy for the second year in succession, having won the 2019 game on penalties

programme.title: Sheridan Smith: Becoming Mum
programme.desc: Documentary following the actress through the course of her pregnancy, as she reflects on her past mental health struggles and fears she may develop post-natal depression. programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.desc: Black Panther teams up with the Avengers to put a stop to Erik Killmonger's evil scheme to steal Vibranium from Wakanda.

programme.title: Drop Dead Weird
programme.desc: Comedy drama following the adventures of an Australian family who move to an isolated seaside village in western Ireland, with the parents having a secret to hide

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by guests Marvin Humes and Suzi Perry, and chefs Daniel Clifford and Bettina Campolucci Bordi. James serves up meatballs, soufflé, quiche and chicken chipotle, as well as providing a Masterclass in honeycomb

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott is joined by Alison Hammond and Aldo Zilli in the first episode of a new cookery series, which focuses on dishes that conjure up memories of holidays. Ainsley prepares spicy Seafood linguine with chilli and cherry tomatoes, Alison makes a Tunisian inspired lamb and cous cous dish, and Aldo gives a masterclass in making crispy chilli and parsley calamari. Finally, Ainsley also whips up a souvlaki and tiramisu

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef visits the region of Jura, where a local farmer shows him around his orchards and teaches him about the region's apples and pears, which James uses as the accompaniment in a barbecued duck. He also visits Fort des Rousses, the old military stronghold that is now used for the making of Comte, one of the country's most popular cheeses

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of this afternoon's meetings, including the 2.35 September Stakes from Kempton Park, the 4.05 Sprint Cup from Haydock Park and action from Ascot. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Adele Mulrennan, reports by Matt Chapman, Sally Ann Grassick and Oli Bell, and commentary from Mark Johnson

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Rolling in It
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a game show featuring a giant arcade machine, which three members of the public have to play to win thousands of pounds. In the final episode, Stephen is joined by guests Mark Wright, Fleur East and Samia Longchambon

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Perry Fenwick, Jenny Eclair, Ore Oduba and Richard Madeley pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the first semi-final of this year's talent contest, making a return to screens following the auditions episodes broadcast in the spring. Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, David Walliams and Ashley Banjo are on the judging desk, as the acts that impressed them the most return, including the golden buzzer acts - singer Fayth Ifil, comedian and singer Jon Courtney, choir Sign Along With Us, comedian Nabil Abdulrashid, and mother and daughter singing duo, Honey and Sammy. Eight acts perform in each semi-final, with two finalists selected by the judges and the votes of viewers

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Vinnie Jones joins Piers Morgan for an intimate Life Stories special, 10 years on from his first appearance on the show. Vinnie opens up about how his life has changed irrevocably in that time, as he comes to terms with grieving for the loss of his wife, Tanya, who passed away last year, after a six-year battle with cancer. He also looks back at some of the highlights of his football and movie careers, talks about his struggles with alcohol, how he is hoping to help other people, particularly men, to cope with grief and what the future now holds

programme.title: UEFA Nations League Highlights
programme.desc: Iceland v England. Mark Pougatch presents action from both teams' opening Group A2 encounter at Laugardalsvollur in Reykjavík. Plus, the key moments from Denmark v Belgium and Sweden v France. With analysis from Ian Wright and Roy Keane and reports by Gabriel Clarke, Richard Henwood and Celina Hinchcliffe, and commentary from Sam Matterface, Lee Dixon, Joe Speight and Seb Hutchinson

programme.title: James Nesbitt: A Game of Two Halves
programme.desc: The accomplished actor embarks on an epic journey to see how Soccer Aid for Unicef is helping children around the world. Since 2006, Soccer Aid has raised over £38million and James will see just how far that money goes. His mission is to take essential supplies to children living in three extreme locations - the Himalayas in Nepal, the desert surrounding the Dead Sea and a remote island in the South Pacific. For James however, the journey is one into the unknown, as he won't know where he is going on each leg of his mission until just before he leaves

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage eight of the most prestigious race on the calendar, which featured a mountainous 141km route from Cazeres-sur-Garonne to Loudenvielle. programme.desc: Ranvir Singh and Alex Beresford visit the country's most fascinating attractions, sharing extraordinary stories of human endeavour and must-see places. programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh presents the first in a series of special episodes in which he reveals the secrets behind some of his favourite garden designs, and looks back on some of the most memorable assignments featured on the show. The first episode focuses on country gardens, and revisits three past projects the team put inventive spins on - a classic country garden, a design with a cottage twist, and finally a project that set out to create an enchanted atmosphere inspired by classic children's book The Secret Garden

programme.title: Never Say Never Again (1983)
programme.desc: James Bond investigates the hijacking of two American nuclear warheads and tracks the stolen missiles to the Bahamas, where a sinister millionaire working for criminal organisation Spectre is planning to hold the world to ransom. programme.title: Soccer Aid
programme.desc: England v Soccer Aid World XI (Kick-off TBA). Dermot O'Leary and Kirsty Gallacher present the pre-match build-up and full live coverage of the pro-celebrity charity football match to raise money in aid of international charity Unicef, which takes place this year at Old Trafford. The England side announced so far includes Joel Dommett, Marvin Humes, Danny Jones, Olly Murs, Joe Wicks and Mark Wright, backed up by legends Wes Brown, Katie Chapman, Andy Cole, David James and Kelly Smith. They will be managed by Sam Allardyce and Wayne Rooney. The World XI side includes Patrice Evra, Yaya Toure, Jason Manford, Iain Stirling, Chelcee Grimes and Kem Cetinay, and they will be hoping to lift the trophy for the second year in succession, having won the 2019 game on penalties

programme.title: Sheridan Smith: Becoming Mum
programme.desc: Documentary following the actress through the course of her pregnancy, as she reflects on her past mental health struggles and fears she may develop post-natal depression. The programme follows Sheridan and her partner as she attends her first scans and pre-natal classes, and meets a psychiatrist who specialises in mental health during and after pregnancy

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: For the final leg of his journey along the Wales Coast Path, Sean Fletcher explores the sand dunes at Merthyr Mawr on horseback. He also discovers how sea cliffs breathe in and out with the pounding of waves and re-creates Marconi's first radio transmission over open water, from Lavernock Point to Flatholm island

programme.desc: Peter and his friends fight to save their favourite booth in the Drunken Clam after it is claimed by another group of men. Meanwhile, Brian passes on herpes to Stewie. Adult animation, featuring the guest voice of Bryan Cranston (Breaking Bad)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter takes Stewie to the park for some father-son bonding, but ends up bringing home someone else's baby. After Lois chastises him for being so stupid, he goes to Chicago on a business trip and returns an intelligent, classy person

programme.title: Family Guy

programme.desc: Cleveland returns to Quahog, but he and Peter are not allowed to spend time together after Lois and Donna get into a big fight over their parenting styles

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan decides to alleviate the stresses of his hectic family life by buying a hot tub. However, he fails to count on his new purchase having an evil agenda of its own. Animated comedy, with the voice of show creator Seth MacFarlane, and guest star Cee-Lo Green

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland thanks Tim for getting him a job in his office, but when he proves a natural in the role and becomes top salesman, jealousy threatens their friendship. Meanwhile, Rallo and Roberta help Cleveland Jr be more sensitive

programme.title: The Cleveland Show

programme.desc: Ellen is joined by talk-show host Steve Harvey, while Zoe Kravitz chats about her new TV comedy drama High Fidelity. Ray Romano plays Ellen's Burning Questions

programme.desc: Volunteers from Ellen's studio audience play her outrageously fun games including Don't Leave Me Hanging, Aw Snap!, Oh Ship! and One-Eyed Monster.

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Banana, Mouse, Frog, Taco and Kitty perform for guest judge Gabriel Iglesias and the rest of the panel. Nick Cannon presents as one of the singers is unmasked and sent home.

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: n London, model Elesha is hoping to be impressed by the style choices of three potential suitors. To impress her, they have to dress her, as Elesha has no other information about them but the outfits they buy. At the end of the challenge, which of the three competitors will she choose to take out on a date?

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness continues to arrange more dates for his 30 single women. This time, the 'love lift' delivers hypnotist Ben from London, Essex-based broker Harry, Edinburgh landscape gardener David and Paul, a 'body double' from Dublin. All four men are given a chance to win over Paddy's singletons, but to secure a date they must first get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval. Plus, an update on how last weeks' couples got on during their stay on the Isle of Fernandos

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring everything from giddy grandmas, cute kids and crazy animals to below-the-belt mishaps, practical jokers and painful-looking accidents

programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, including a quad bike that ruins a wedding, an agoraphobic dog and a sensitive cat that hates being called fat

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Lyndsey threatens to end things with Alan when he refuses to give her a key to the house, while Walden, Herb and Billy commiserate about their failed relationships. Comedy, starring Ashton Kutcher, Jon Cryer and Courtney Thorne

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Jake returns from the base with his girlfriend Tammy, and asks Walden for money so he can propose to her. However, the teenager has misunderstood his relationship with the tattoo shop owner, who has several children and just wants a bit of fun with no strings attached. Comedy, starring Ashton Kutcher, Jon Cryer and Angus T Jones

programme.title: Love Island USA
programme.desc: US version of the hit reality show featuring a gang of gorgeous young singles gathered in one villa for the time of their lives. Summer's almost over but things are just starting to heat up, and the brand-new batch of Islanders move into a stunning villa, each one looking for a real romantic connection

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois gets a job as a postal worker to pay for Stewie's private school and finds an unsent letter from Peter that was meant for an old flame of his

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Chris interrupt Stewie's TV time, prompting the angry baby to go back in time to break up Peter and Lois so he's never conceived. However, he is instead reborn into a Downton Abbey-esque British household

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Steve tries to prove his toughness to Stan by adopting the persona of a superhero, while Francine also goes through an identity crisis when she receives news that could change the family for ever

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan finally saves enough money to join the golf club he has worked at for the past 30 summers - only to discover Steve has been given a membership before him. However, everything changes when the CIA officer learns who owns the business. Featuring the voice of Hulk Hogan

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland visits his old high school and learns there is no longer a baseball team, prompting him to raise money to rebuild the stadium before the season begins. However, he decides to take a more hands-on role in coaching the team when he discovers his son Cleveland Jr is more keen on solving maths riddles than pitching balls

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Roberta's new tutor encourages her to change her appearance to teach her an important lesson. However, the transformation proves a little too successful when Cleveland Jr falls for her

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid takes a little exercise and Trevor decides to join him. Seventies sitcom, starring Sid James and Anthony Jackson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Johnny's past returns to haunt him when Scott tells Jenny all about the crime they committed more than 40 years ago, while temperatures rise at Speed Daal when Geoff turns up for work. Having returned from their meeting, Abi thanks Peter for his support, but Debbie is watching from a distance, with her suspicions heightened

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alya is furious to find an alcohol delivery on the doorstep of Speed Daal and refuses point blank to let Geoff buy her out of the business, leading him to resort to sabotage. David arranges to take Shona for a walk, but it doesn't go quite to plan, while Daniel apologises to Ray for causing a scene in the bistro

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff plays the victim once again, but Tim wonders if his dad is as innocent as he makes out, while Mary and Cathy discuss Dev's money problems. Daniel admits to Adam that he's been paying Nicky to dress up as Sinead so he could pretend she's still alive, and David assures Shona that she's improving all the time

programme.title: The Great St Trinian's Train Robbery (1966)
programme.desc: A crook and his cronies stash the money from a train heist in a deserted old mansion, planning to recover it in good time. However, several years later, when the thieves arrive to collect their loot, they discover the building has been turned into a school for girls where the pupils have become infamous for their escapades. Comedy, starring Frankie Howerd, Dora Bryan, Reg Varney and George Cole

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in Captain Hastings' garage on Bonfire Night. It transpires that the deceased was due to marry an MP - and although the evidence suggests suicide, the Belgian detective suspects foul play. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Schoolgirl Jennifer Sutcliffe becomes embroiled in a mystery when priceless jewels are hidden among her belongings. The precious stones once belonged to a prince who fell foul of a revolution, and now his cousin is a pupil at the same school as Jennifer, where Poirot finds himself drawn into the unlikely situation and realises nothing is quite what it seems. David Suchet stars as the Belgian sleuth, with Harriet Walter and Claire Skinner

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Oxford's last-surviving all-female college is holding a reunion to bid farewell to one of its most prominent professors. However, the evening leads to tragedy when a guest is found murdered, and Lewis is convinced the killing is connected to an attack that occurred at the same institution 10 years previously. The detective calls in his former DS, Alison McLennan, to help with the investigation, but the case takes a twist when two more bodies turn up. Crime drama, guest starring Juliet Stevenson and Saskia Reeves, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The crime drama returns, with Barnaby and Winter joined on the year's first case by a new pathologist, Petra Antonescu (Anamaria Marinca). A woman slips away from a Jane Austen re-enactment in full costume, only to be stabbed to death in the woods - her murder being recorded by a camera drone. The victim is discovered to be an undercover journalist, who had some unusual questions to ask about the village and the country house where the historical event took place. Guest starring Claire Skinner and Samuel West

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Joyce Barnaby swerves off the road into a ditch, convinced she has hit someone, but nobody is found injured and Tom thinks she has imagined it. However, when a local historian discovers the body of a librarian in a nearby cemetery, Joyce blames herself for the man's death, leading her husband into a spooky investigation to find the real killer on the loose in the haunted village of March Magna. Guest starring Danny Webb (The Bill), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: When a student from a prestigious all-female college is found dead, Will and Geordie infiltrate the murky world of campus politics and university societies to find the killer. Navigating strange initiations, raucous parties - and with investigative journalist, Ellie Harding, hot on their heels - they need to use every advantage at their disposal to unravel the mystery. Cathy's ambitions for a life of her own start to test the delicate family balance, while fresh from a spell with Daniel in Marrakech, Leonard finds it hard to acclimatise to life back in Grantchester. Robson Green and Tom Brittney star

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: After Will and Leonard witness a deadly and deliberate hit-and-run, Geordie traces the car to a pair of decidedly dysfunctional brothers, whose house lies far off the beaten path. The victim's wife, his employee and the two brothers are all damaged characters caught in a web of lies and betrayals - and it's not just secrets hiding within the siblings' grounds. Back at home, Will and Geordie both struggle as unwelcome guests look set to stay - Will's mum Amelia seems to have found a carbon copy of his late father in her new beau. Meanwhile, a new television set lands in the vicarage - just in time for Eurovision. Robson Green, Tom Brittney and Al Weaver star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The murder of a caretaker has been so carefully planned and executed that the police are left without a single piece of evidence. Adding to the mystery is the fact that the crime is laid out in detail in a manual purchased three weeks earlier in the victim's name. Brooks and Casey eventually track down the killer, who confesses, but the trial is put in jeopardy by a canny defence lawyer. Guest starring Toby Stephens

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A murder inquiry is launched when the police are alerted to shocking internet footage showing a teenage girl being attacked and shot. The investigation is hindered by the lack of a body, but the video provides enough clues to eventually put a name to the victim and track down her killers. However, a vow of silence between the defendants means Jake and Alesha face one of their toughest court battles yet

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The team tries to juggle three cases over the course of one day, beginning at 6am when Brooks and Casey are called to a crime scene where a barman has been shot dead in his car. Then, a family dispute leads to murder, and the frenetic morning takes an unexpected turn when an allegation of police intimidation is made against one of the officers. Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike takes up photography, and persuades Sid to let him use the old garage for his new hobby. Family comedy, starring Sid James, Diana Coupland and Robin Stewart

programme.desc: Mandy is still determined to show Vinny that Paul cannot be trusted, while Cain returns from his trip, but his mood darkens upon discovering that Belle is back with Jamie. Billy is released from police custody and interrogated by Will, and Tracy encourages Nate to organise a family meeting so they can give everyone the news about the baby

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Moira receives a call from the police informing her they have found a new witness to her hit and run, while an emotional Paul assures Vinny that nothing will force them apart again. Marlon is riled when Al stops over at the Woolpack to gloat over the Hide's takeaway service success, and Nate lashes out, smashing Jamie's car wing

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: A worn-down Amy reveals to Marlon that Al's threatening to sack her because of a missing delivery of pizza boxes, while Jamie overhears Sam bad-mouthing him. The social worker enters in the midst of a row between Charity and Sarah, and Belle arrives back at the Dingle's to collect her things after confessing to Zak that she's moving in with Jamie

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A former bookie falls under suspicion for the murder of a hospital benefactor, who is found dead in his car. As the detectives investigate the case, it becomes clear their prime suspect had a strong motive, believing the victim was responsible for putting his daughter into a coma. Brian Cox guest stars, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An opera singer is shot by a sniper at an Oxford University ceremony just as she is about to receive an honorary degree. Morse is a big fan of her work and thinks she could not possibly be involved in anything untoward, but his investigations turn up more than a few sordid secrets. Detective drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Robert Hardy, Sheila Gish, John Gielgud and Rachel Weisz

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. There seems to be no motive for murder, and no trace of poison found in the victim's glass, but weeks later, when Sir Charles's doctor friend Sir Bartholomew Strange dies in similar circumstances, he and Poirot team up to investigate. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate the murder of a businesswoman connected with an organisation that supports gifted children, who had apparently been blackmailing the father of one particularly promising youngster. Further deaths follow, and as the body count rises, they realise there is a decades-old secret at the heart of the case. Matters are complicated still further by the presence in Oxford of former MI5 spymaster Grace Orde, who is visiting to sign copies of her tell-all memoir - which provides a vital clue to the killer's identity. Detective drama, guest starring Anna Chancellor and Cherie Lunghi, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Musicians compete for a prestigious award at the annual Thassingham Classical Music Festival - and Barnaby and Winter are called in when the winner is brutally murdered and his priceless violin is stolen. Suspicion rapidly falls on the maestro organising the festival (James Fleet), who had planned to debut his new composition at the event and has stirred up resentment by turning a community festival into a corporate one - while many suspect that the murder is the result of a curse. However, a drunken viola player (Simon Callow) turns out to have some dark secrets that may implicate him in the crime

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: Major documentary telling the inside story of the royal family and the Queen's record-breaking reign. The first episode focuses on the conflict between love and duty that faces the royals - and which has occasionally threatened to bring down the House of Windsor. Edward VIII fatefully chose love when he abdicated to marry divorcee Wallis Simpson. Charles chose duty when he married Diana. The Queen, however, has managed to find a balance, maintaining a long marriage while becoming the longest-serving monarch

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: As the Sanditon summer regatta approaches, Sidney wrestles with his feelings towards an old flame and Young Stringer reveals his feelings to Charlotte. With Lady Denham at death's door, the tensions between Edward, Clara and Esther finally come to a head - with surprising consequences. Penultimate episode of Andrew Davies's adaptation of the unfinished final novel by Jane Austen about a spirited young woman who finds herself in a seaside resort on the cusp of dramatic change. Starring Rose Williams, Theo James and Anne Reid

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: Becky is left alone in the world and living in greatly reduced circumstances when Steyne arranges for Rawdon to be shipped overseas. Dobbin discovers that Amelia plans to remarry, a revelation that inspires him to finally confess his feelings for her - but he is not prepared for her reaction. The Sedley family takes a holiday in Germany, where they are unexpectedly reunited with Becky - who gets the chance to redeem a lifetime of selfishness by performing a single good deed on behalf of her dearest friend

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A police lab technician is stabbed to death in her home and while Brooks and Casey pursue their lead suspect, another woman is attacked in an identical fashion. The second victim survives and is able to identify her assailant, but she and Casey fall for each other - a relationship that threatens to damage both the CPS's case and the detective's career. Guest starring Lydia Leonard, with Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: In Dover, Lieutenant Colin Race is investigating the deaths of two Navy personnel when a distraught Sheila Webb runs out of 19 Wilbraham Crescent and into his arms. Inside the house, Colin finds the body of a murder victim surrounded by stopped clocks. Mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Massey, Jaime Winstone, Lesley Sharp, Phil Daniels, Geoffrey Palmer and Tessa Peake-Jones

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A wartime memento prompts Sid and Jean to visit Paris. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland
