programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers take in a lodger, and Mildred is delighted when he begins doing all the household jobs that George will not tackle. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A guest at a village Halloween party boasts of having witnessed a murder years previously - and later meets a grisly end in keeping with the macabre spirit of the occasion. Arriving to investigate, Poirot delves back into the annals of local life - and uncovers a series of mysterious deaths, any one of which could have a bearing on the present case. David Suchet, Amelia Bullmore and Zoe Wanamaker star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An audacious thief steals the Aidensfield force's panda car and carries out a series of brazen robberies disguised as a police officer, culminating in a serious break-in at a jeweller's shop. Meanwhile, Vernon persuades Lord Ashfordly to host an exclusive painting weekend and offers his expertise to the amateur artists

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank finds someone has slashed his tyres and written the word 'murderer' on his car. Alan makes Terry an offer he can't refuse. The Cairns struggle with their financial problems

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Cairns worry about paying for building work on Woodside Farm. Biff tells Kathy that Linda is pregnant. Terry tells Alan that he has unbarred the Dingles

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des struggles to get used to having a full house. Liz is horrified to find out the cost of her divorce bill. Sally is annoyed when Ida admits to clocking Tricia in at the factory

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tricia tries again to contact Terry. Stephen tells the Platts he'd like to take them to Canada. Becky is upset when she hears that Liz is Des's old flame

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with old friend Ariadne Oliver, who has been commissioned to stage a game of murder hunt at a summer fete in the grounds of Nasse House, the recently purchased home of wealthy financier Sir George Stubbs. However, the novelist's instincts tell her she's getting involved in something much darker - and she's proved right when the girl volunteering to play the part of the victim really does turn out to be dead. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Zoe Wanamaker, Sean Pertwee, Martin Jarvis, Sam Kelly, Sinead Cusack, Rosalind Ayres and Tom Ellis

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An audacious thief steals the Aidensfield force's panda car and carries out a series of brazen robberies disguised as a police officer, culminating in a serious break-in at a jeweller's shop. Meanwhile, Vernon persuades Lord Ashfordly to host an exclusive painting weekend and offers his expertise to the amateur artists

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Businessman and owner of the Causton Lions rugby team Mark Adler is mysteriously found dead in the club's cryotherapy chamber, which is normally used by the players for physiotherapy. Barnaby and Winter are called in to investigate and their list of suspects soon includes Adler's wife Samantha, former coach Guy Bevan, troubled star player Danny Wickham and physio Neville Gallagher. Neil Dudgeon stars, with Tamzin Outhwaite, Don Gilet and Michael Maloney

programme.title: DCI Banks: Aftermath
programme.desc: Two-part drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson and based on the novel by Peter Robinson. DCI Alan Banks investigates the most difficult case of his life when five girls go missing but only four bodies are found. Chief suspect Marcus Payne is in a coma, and ambitious DS Annie Cabbot from the Professional Standards department pursues Banks in an effort to find out why. Andrea Lowe and Charlotte Riley also star

programme.title: Little Boy Blue
programme.desc: Fact-based crime drama about the fatal shooting of 11-year-old Rhys Jones as he walked home from football practice in Croxteth, Liverpool, caught in the crossfire of a feud between rival gangs. Detective Superintendent Dave Kelly is charged with bringing his teenage killer to justice, but though the assailant's identity is well known, finding the evidence to charge him proves to be a far more difficult challenge. Stephen Graham stars

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The peace of an Oxfordshire village is shattered by the apparently motiveless shooting of a woman through her kitchen window. Twenty-four hours later, her next-door neighbour suffers a similar fate. As Morse searches for a link between the victims, he becomes embroiled in an increasingly bitter election contest at an Oxford college. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Richard Briers, Maggie Steed and John Shrapnel

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean encounters an old flame, prompting a jealous Sid to remember why he married her in the first place. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid takes a little exercise and Trevor decides to join him. Seventies sitcom, starring Sid James and Anthony Jackson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Johnny's past returns to haunt him when Scott tells Jenny all about the crime they committed more than 40 years ago, while temperatures rise at Speed Daal when Geoff turns up for work. Having returned from their meeting, Abi thanks Peter for his support, but Debbie is watching from a distance, with her suspicions heightened

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alya is furious to find an alcohol delivery on the doorstep of Speed Daal and refuses point blank to let Geoff buy her out of the business, leading him to resort to sabotage. David arranges to take Shona for a walk, but it doesn't go quite to plan, while Daniel apologises to Ray for causing a scene in the bistro

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff plays the victim once again, but Tim wonders if his dad is as innocent as he makes out, while Mary and Cathy discuss Dev's money problems. Daniel admits to Adam that he's been paying Nicky to dress up as Sinead so he could pretend she's still alive, and David assures Shona that she's improving all the time

programme.title: The Great St Trinian's Train Robbery (1966)
programme.desc: A crook and his cronies stash the money from a train heist in a deserted old mansion, planning to recover it in good time. However, several years later, when the thieves arrive to collect their loot, they discover the building has been turned into a school for girls where the pupils have become infamous for their escapades. Comedy, starring Frankie Howerd, Dora Bryan, Reg Varney and George Cole

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in Captain Hastings' garage on Bonfire Night. It transpires that the deceased was due to marry an MP - and although the evidence suggests suicide, the Belgian detective suspects foul play. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Schoolgirl Jennifer Sutcliffe becomes embroiled in a mystery when priceless jewels are hidden among her belongings. The precious stones once belonged to a prince who fell foul of a revolution, and now his cousin is a pupil at the same school as Jennifer, where Poirot finds himself drawn into the unlikely situation and realises nothing is quite what it seems. David Suchet stars as the Belgian sleuth, with Harriet Walter and Claire Skinner

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Oxford's last-surviving all-female college is holding a reunion to bid farewell to one of its most prominent professors. However, the evening leads to tragedy when a guest is found murdered, and Lewis is convinced the killing is connected to an attack that occurred at the same institution 10 years previously. The detective calls in his former DS, Alison McLennan, to help with the investigation, but the case takes a twist when two more bodies turn up. Crime drama, guest starring Juliet Stevenson and Saskia Reeves, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The crime drama returns, with Barnaby and Winter joined on the year's first case by a new pathologist, Petra Antonescu (Anamaria Marinca). A woman slips away from a Jane Austen re-enactment in full costume, only to be stabbed to death in the woods - her murder being recorded by a camera drone. The victim is discovered to be an undercover journalist, who had some unusual questions to ask about the village and the country house where the historical event took place. Guest starring Claire Skinner and Samuel West

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Joyce Barnaby swerves off the road into a ditch, convinced she has hit someone, but nobody is found injured and Tom thinks she has imagined it. However, when a local historian discovers the body of a librarian in a nearby cemetery, Joyce blames herself for the man's death, leading her husband into a spooky investigation to find the real killer on the loose in the haunted village of March Magna. Guest starring Danny Webb (The Bill), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: When a student from a prestigious all-female college is found dead, Will and Geordie infiltrate the murky world of campus politics and university societies to find the killer. Navigating strange initiations, raucous parties - and with investigative journalist, Ellie Harding, hot on their heels - they need to use every advantage at their disposal to unravel the mystery. Cathy's ambitions for a life of her own start to test the delicate family balance, while fresh from a spell with Daniel in Marrakech, Leonard finds it hard to acclimatise to life back in Grantchester. Robson Green and Tom Brittney star

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: After Will and Leonard witness a deadly and deliberate hit-and-run, Geordie traces the car to a pair of decidedly dysfunctional brothers, whose house lies far off the beaten path. The victim's wife, his employee and the two brothers are all damaged characters caught in a web of lies and betrayals - and it's not just secrets hiding within the siblings' grounds. Back at home, Will and Geordie both struggle as unwelcome guests look set to stay - Will's mum Amelia seems to have found a carbon copy of his late father in her new beau. Meanwhile, a new television set lands in the vicarage - just in time for Eurovision. Robson Green, Tom Brittney and Al Weaver star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The murder of a caretaker has been so carefully planned and executed that the police are left without a single piece of evidence. Adding to the mystery is the fact that the crime is laid out in detail in a manual purchased three weeks earlier in the victim's name. Brooks and Casey eventually track down the killer, who confesses, but the trial is put in jeopardy by a canny defence lawyer. Guest starring Toby Stephens

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A murder inquiry is launched when the police are alerted to shocking internet footage showing a teenage girl being attacked and shot. The investigation is hindered by the lack of a body, but the video provides enough clues to eventually put a name to the victim and track down her killers. However, a vow of silence between the defendants means Jake and Alesha face one of their toughest court battles yet

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The team tries to juggle three cases over the course of one day, beginning at 6am when Brooks and Casey are called to a crime scene where a barman has been shot dead in his car. Then, a family dispute leads to murder, and the frenetic morning takes an unexpected turn when an allegation of police intimidation is made against one of the officers. Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth takes a break on the island of Rhodes, where an English beauty draws attention by flirting with fellow guests at the Palace Hotel. When she is found murdered, her husband is immediately suspected, but Poirot quickly deduces there is more to the case than a simple crime of passion. Whodunit, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike takes up photography, and persuades Sid to let him use the old garage for his new hobby. Family comedy, starring Sid James, Diana Coupland and Robin Stewart

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The Abbotts catch flu and Sid is forced to act as nursemaid. Sitcom, starring Sid James

David arranges to take Shona for a walk, but it doesn't go quite to plan, while Daniel apologises to Ray for causing a scene in the bistro

Daniel admits to Adam that he's been paying Nicky to dress up as Sinead so he could pretend she's still alive, and David assures Shona that she's improving all the time

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy is still determined to show Vinny that Paul cannot be trusted, while Cain returns from his trip, but his mood darkens upon discovering that Belle is back with Jamie. Billy is released from police custody and interrogated by Will, and Tracy encourages Nate to organise a family meeting so they can give everyone the news about the baby

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Moira receives a call from the police informing her they have found a new witness to her hit and run, while an emotional Paul assures Vinny that nothing will force them apart again. Marlon is riled when Al stops over at the Woolpack to gloat over the Hide's takeaway service success, and Nate lashes out, smashing Jamie's car wing

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: A worn-down Amy reveals to Marlon that Al's threatening to sack her because of a missing delivery of pizza boxes, while Jamie overhears Sam bad-mouthing him. The social worker enters in the midst of a row between Charity and Sarah, and Belle arrives back at the Dingle's to collect her things after confessing to Zak that she's moving in with Jamie

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A former bookie falls under suspicion for the murder of a hospital benefactor, who is found dead in his car. As the detectives investigate the case, it becomes clear their prime suspect had a strong motive, believing the victim was responsible for putting his daughter into a coma. Brian Cox guest stars, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An opera singer is shot by a sniper at an Oxford University ceremony just as she is about to receive an honorary degree. Morse is a big fan of her work and thinks she could not possibly be involved in anything untoward, but his investigations turn up more than a few sordid secrets. Detective drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Robert Hardy, Sheila Gish, John Gielgud and Rachel Weisz

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. There seems to be no motive for murder, and no trace of poison found in the victim's glass, but weeks later, when Sir Charles's doctor friend Sir Bartholomew Strange dies in similar circumstances, he and Poirot team up to investigate. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate the murder of a businesswoman connected with an organisation that supports gifted children, who had apparently been blackmailing the father of one particularly promising youngster. Further deaths follow, and as the body count rises, they realise there is a decades-old secret at the heart of the case. Matters are complicated still further by the presence in Oxford of former MI5 spymaster Grace Orde, who is visiting to sign copies of her tell-all memoir - which provides a vital clue to the killer's identity. Detective drama, guest starring Anna Chancellor and Cherie Lunghi, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Musicians compete for a prestigious award at the annual Thassingham Classical Music Festival - and Barnaby and Winter are called in when the winner is brutally murdered and his priceless violin is stolen. Suspicion rapidly falls on the maestro organising the festival (James Fleet), who had planned to debut his new composition at the event and has stirred up resentment by turning a community festival into a corporate one - while many suspect that the murder is the result of a curse. However, a drunken viola player (Simon Callow) turns out to have some dark secrets that may implicate him in the crime

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: Major documentary telling the inside story of the royal family and the Queen's record-breaking reign. The first episode focuses on the conflict between love and duty that faces the royals - and which has occasionally threatened to bring down the House of Windsor. Edward VIII fatefully chose love when he abdicated to marry divorcee Wallis Simpson. Charles chose duty when he married Diana. The Queen, however, has managed to find a balance, maintaining a long marriage while becoming the longest-serving monarch

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: As the Sanditon summer regatta approaches, Sidney wrestles with his feelings towards an old flame and Young Stringer reveals his feelings to Charlotte. With Lady Denham at death's door, the tensions between Edward, Clara and Esther finally come to a head - with surprising consequences. Penultimate episode of Andrew Davies's adaptation of the unfinished final novel by Jane Austen about a spirited young woman who finds herself in a seaside resort on the cusp of dramatic change. Starring Rose Williams, Theo James and Anne Reid

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: Becky is left alone in the world and living in greatly reduced circumstances when Steyne arranges for Rawdon to be shipped overseas. Dobbin discovers that Amelia plans to remarry, a revelation that inspires him to finally confess his feelings for her - but he is not prepared for her reaction. The Sedley family takes a holiday in Germany, where they are unexpectedly reunited with Becky - who gets the chance to redeem a lifetime of selfishness by performing a single good deed on behalf of her dearest friend

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A police lab technician is stabbed to death in her home and while Brooks and Casey pursue their lead suspect, another woman is attacked in an identical fashion. The second victim survives and is able to identify her assailant, but she and Casey fall for each other - a relationship that threatens to damage both the CPS's case and the detective's career. Guest starring Lydia Leonard, with Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: In Dover, Lieutenant Colin Race is investigating the deaths of two Navy personnel when a distraught Sheila Webb runs out of 19 Wilbraham Crescent and into his arms. Inside the house, Colin finds the body of a murder victim surrounded by stopped clocks. Mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Massey, Jaime Winstone, Lesley Sharp, Phil Daniels, Geoffrey Palmer and Tessa Peake-Jones

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A wartime memento prompts Sid and Jean to visit Paris. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.desc: A wartime memento prompts Sid and Jean to visit Paris. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee

programme.title: Tomorrow Never Dies (1997)

programme.title: Death Race (2008)

Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The youthful heir to a fortune disappears from boarding school in the dead of night - but his aristocratic father's aversion to publicity makes the case particularly difficult to investigate. Period drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Mrs Peel is kidnapped by a mad Teutonic film director, who is attempting to make a film about her life. Unfortunately for Peel it ends with her death, leaving Steed in a hurry to save her. Patrick Macnee and Diana Rigg star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is conned into buying an East German car, a retired greyhound and a piano. He fears the items could destroy his reputation, leaving him with the job of selling them on without the rest of the manor finding out. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is given a deadly new rifle for testing, but the weapon is promptly stolen. To make matters worse, he discovers he is being stalked by an old friend who has embarked on a vendetta. Vicki Michelle ('Allo 'Allo!) makes an early screen appearance, alongside Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage nine of the most prestigious race on the calendar, which took the riders on a mountainous 153km route from Pau to Laruns. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: Paul is kidnapped and Harry and the Contessa are forced to assist in a theft in order to save him. Drama, guest starring Peter Bowles, with Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Tony Anholt

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The prime minister asks Holmes to investigate the disappearance of a letter which could lead to a major European catastrophe. Unfortunately, completion of the task requires him to jeopardise a titled lady's reputation - something he is naturally reluctant to do. Victorian detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed is invited to a party on an aeroplane with a motley crew. However, as the pilotless craft takes off, a voice tells them they are bound for a desert island where one will be revealed as a killer. Donald Sutherland, Charlotte Rampling and Brian Blessed guest star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur spies a business opportunity as he and Ray prepare to pay their respects to the late Charlie Johnson, but the deceased's widow has other ideas. Comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: The brother of a suspected drug-dealer dies while in custody, and his elder sibling directs his powerful lawyer to instigate a committee to investigate CI5's involvement. With the future of the division in question, Bodie and Doyle try to prove the family's drug-trade connection and the real reason behind the man's death. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A frustrated housewife supplies Regan with the names of the gang who recently ransacked a bookmakers - including that of her husband, a notorious robber. However, he has a cast-iron alibi, so proving his guilt proves harder than anticipated. Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Gary Imlach presents a look back at the opening nine stages as the race reached its first rest day. Plus, reports from the riders' press conferences and a look ahead to week two. With analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: After a mystery sea monster washes up on a UK beach, extreme angler Jeremy Wade sets off on a journey to unmask this beast and find out whether it poses any threat to humans. His journey takes him to London, where he digs for clues among celebrated naturalist Charles Darwin's historic specimens, to the frozen waters of Greenland, where he ice fishes deeper than he's ever fished before, and on to the fjords of Norway, where he attempts to reel in one of these sea monsters before an Arctic storm rolls in

programme.desc: The team travel to a venue in Hungary that has produced carp weighing more than 40kg in the past. However, they quickly discover it shares a common problem with many lakes in this part of the world - there are also hordes of hungry smaller carp under 20lb

programme.desc: A raw recruit is thrown into the thick of the Vietnam War along the Cambodian border, where he finds himself caught up in a lethal feud between two sergeants. programme.desc: Arthur is conned into buying an East German car, a retired greyhound and a piano. He fears the items could destroy his reputation, leaving him with the job of selling them on without the rest of the manor finding out. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is given a deadly new rifle for testing, but the weapon is promptly stolen. To make matters worse, he discovers he is being stalked by an old friend who has embarked on a vendetta. Vicki Michelle ('Allo 'Allo!) makes an early screen appearance, alongside Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage nine of the most prestigious race on the calendar, which took the riders on a mountainous 153km route from Pau to Laruns. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: Paul is kidnapped and Harry and the Contessa are forced to assist in a theft in order to save him. Drama, guest starring Peter Bowles, with Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Tony Anholt

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The prime minister asks Holmes to investigate the disappearance of a letter which could lead to a major European catastrophe. Unfortunately, completion of the task requires him to jeopardise a titled lady's reputation - something he is naturally reluctant to do. Victorian detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed is invited to a party on an aeroplane with a motley crew. However, as the pilotless craft takes off, a voice tells them they are bound for a desert island where one will be revealed as a killer. Donald Sutherland, Charlotte Rampling and Brian Blessed guest star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur spies a business opportunity as he and Ray prepare to pay their respects to the late Charlie Johnson, but the deceased's widow has other ideas. Comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: The brother of a suspected drug-dealer dies while in custody, and his elder sibling directs his powerful lawyer to instigate a committee to investigate CI5's involvement. With the future of the division in question, Bodie and Doyle try to prove the family's drug-trade connection and the real reason behind the man's death. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A frustrated housewife supplies Regan with the names of the gang who recently ransacked a bookmakers - including that of her husband, a notorious robber. However, he has a cast-iron alibi, so proving his guilt proves harder than anticipated. Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Gary Imlach presents a look back at the opening nine stages as the race reached its first rest day. Plus, reports from the riders' press conferences and a look ahead to week two. With analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: After a mystery sea monster washes up on a UK beach, extreme angler Jeremy Wade sets off on a journey to unmask this beast and find out whether it poses any threat to humans. His journey takes him to London, where he digs for clues among celebrated naturalist Charles Darwin's historic specimens, to the frozen waters of Greenland, where he ice fishes deeper than he's ever fished before, and on to the fjords of Norway, where he attempts to reel in one of these sea monsters before an Arctic storm rolls in

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: The team travel to a venue in Hungary that has produced carp weighing more than 40kg in the past. However, they quickly discover it shares a common problem with many lakes in this part of the world - there are also hordes of hungry smaller carp under 20lb

programme.title: Formula 1

programme.desc: Debra and Marie shame Ray into giving Robert a second bachelor party to make up for his disappointing first attempt

programme.title: The Simpsons

Comedy, with Parminder Nagra, Keira Knightley, Jonathan Rhys Meyers, Juliet Stevenson and Shaznay Lewis

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: More of the most memorable moments from the past five years, including one of the zoo's most enduring love stories at Penguin Island. At 22, Rudd is the oldest penguin on the island and has been with his partner Spike for more than 15 years. Meanwhile, Silvery gibbon Alvin is keen to get close to his partner Tilu again, but ever since they had a baby together, young Eko has proven a bit of a passion killer

Hamilton stretched his lead at the top of the standings to 47 points with a win last weekend at the Belgian Grand prix, making it five wins from seven this season

programme.title: George Clarke's National Trust Unlocked

He also adds his name to the list of dukes, earls and viscounts who've made the astonishing Grade I-listed Cliveden Gardens their playground over the past 300 years, and investigates the unique home of a fellow architect at Snowshill Manor in the Cotswolds, a treasure trove of more than 22,000 curiosities collected from all around the world

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking Family Favourites
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver gives roast chicken a makeover with a kimchi glaze and sauce, dressing it in toasted sesame seeds and serving with fluffy white rice and a refreshing slaw, to create a truly memorable meal. He then stuffs a salmon fillet with creamy prawns and wraps it in bacon, cooking it on a bed of greens for a traybake fit for family, friends and special occasions, and finishes off by rustling up a super-speedy, all-in-one cake with the flavours of pistachio, tahini and pomegranate

programme.title: The Yorkshire Jobcentre
programme.desc: More from life behind the scenes at a busy public employment service. Penny used to run a multimillion pound retail chain in Nigeria with her husband, but since their divorce the 49-year-old has found herself living in Leeds on universal credit. Lindsey in the social justice team helps Dean prepare for a test which, if he passes, could lead to a job in the construction industry, while Afghan asylum seeker Zaref not only does needs a job, but has to earn more than £18,600 to bring his wife to the U

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the work of police in Northamptonshire, where, with resources stretched to breaking point, officers are struggling to meet the increased demand on their services. This edition looks at the impact of relationships on the force, with last year seeing were more than 13,000 domestic incidents in the county

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Return of the documentary following life inside an A&E department. The series begins with the story of 22-year-old police officer Hazel, who is rushed to St George's after crashing her motorbike while out on a ride with her father. Meanwhile, 86-year-old John is admitted to A&E with a suspected 'triple A' - a ruptured aortic aneurysm in his abdomen, a condition that can be fatal. The doctors must work quickly to diagnose the cause of the internal bleeding, but their investigations reveal further complications. John's children share the story of their father's life, and his surprising secret that sent shockwaves through the family

programme.title: Call the Cops
programme.desc: A look at the challenges the Devon and Cornwall police force face patrolling the 730 miles of coastline in their jurisdiction. Cornwall is famous for a long history of smuggling, but this criminal activity is not a thing of the past. In this episode, the police control room receives a call from a member of the public on Friday morning, claiming unusual cargo is being unloaded from a tiny boat into a waiting van. Soon 30 police units from across both counties have been deployed, but a shocking discovery is made when the vehicle is tracked down

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

Pete maintains he makes the best pizza in town - based on a review from the 1980s - but the fiery chef's own taste test proves the opposite. programme.desc: Harlequins v Bath (Kick-off 2.00pm). Coverage of the match from the 18th round of fixtures, which comes from The Stoop. Stories from the royal household's staff, include a cook revealing the Queen's surprisingly simple favourite treat, and a footman of the Queen Mother recounting his most embarrassing gaffe, involving wine and Princess Margaret. Plus, historian Kate Williams examines some of the most bizarre and shocking servant duties from ancient times

programme.title: Secret Scotland
programme.desc: Comedian Susan Calman takes a lighthearted jaunt around her homeland to uncover the untold tales behind some of Scotland's most famous locations. She visits romantic and rugged locations, and takes an in-depth look at their history, people and exciting treasures, often receiving private tours of iconic Scottish spots and uncovering hidden secrets of the past and present. From quiet countryside to brooding coastlines, this love letter to Scotland demonstrates the best of what the country has to offer

programme.title: The Palace vs the Press: Royals Under Fire
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the relationship the younger members of the royal family have with the media, looking at the power game between a young royal generation who know how to give as good as they get and a press constantly pushing to regain the upper hand in their search for ever more tantalising stories

programme.title: Live Boxing
programme.desc: Samir Ziani v Alex Dilmaghani. Coverage of the bout for the EBU European Super Featherweight title, which comes from Production Park in South Kirkby. Londoner Dilmaghani's last contest was a draw 10 months ago when he drew with Francisco Fonseca, and he will now look to get back to winning ways against the experienced Frenchman, who made a successful defence of the title last October when he outpointed Faroukh Kourbanov

programme.title: Police Code Zero: Officer Under Attack
programme.desc: Programme exploring the rising number of assaults on police officers, revealing what they face in the line of duty, and the emotional toll these incidents take. programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The broadcaster returns to find out how railways have been central to the history of four very different parts of the world. He begins by embarking on a journey across the Alps, starting in Vienna, before navigating one of the worst avalanche zones in the world. He then heads for St Moritz on a railway that was made possible in part by British holiday pioneer Thomas Cook, before boarding two steep cog railways to climb up the north face of the Eiger to reach the 'Roof of Europe'

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A runaway suspect puts Gary's life in danger, Scott and Adam discover a huge stash of drugs in the boot of a car, and officers are called to a New Year's Eve pub brawl. Plus, Ady catches a car thief, and five police vehicles are involved in a motorway chase

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Scott and Dave use GPS technology to track a pair of mobile-phone thieves, and a car crashes into a house during the Christmas period. Plus, Nick pursues a criminal gang, Vicky deals with a van containing suspicious-looking scrap, and Jason contends with his own traffic nightmare

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Landlord Nicholas Parrington discovers a couple are subletting his house, just months after a previous tenant had turned the property into a brothel, while investors in a community centre for children are aghast when squatters move into their building. In Lewisham, the council's Rogue Landlord Unit raids one of their main streets, on the lookout for slum conditions, illegal overcrowding and criminal activities

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: A landlord's tenants refuse to move out, so he starts extensive building work around them, while another landlord tries to recover £30,000 in unpaid rent through the courts

programme.title: 5 News Weekend
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.desc: A one-ton bull needs a foot trim, but is reluctant to lie down for the procedure, so Peter has to approach its feet very carefully to avoid his patient falling on him. Julian and Katy operate on a beagle with damaged ligaments, and a cat undergoes a caesarean section - a procedure that is very rarely used on feline patients. The Greens release their calves and donkeys into the field together for the first time, and a tortoise displays a loss of appetite

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Adaptation of James Herriot's books about life as a vet in the Yorkshire Dales. Fresh out of veterinary college in 1937, James Herriot struggles to find work in his Depression-struck home town of Glasgow. Seeking new opportunities, he pursues a position in the remote village of Darrowby working for eccentric local vet Siegfried Farnon, who is less than impressed by his early efforts. However, a difficult calving gives James a chance to prove his worth. Starring Nicholas Ralph, Samuel West and Diana Rigg

programme.title: Me & My 10 Kids: Mega Families
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary following the hectic lives of two British families wrestling with the struggles and joys of raising 10 kids apiece. Single mum Sonya struggles on a tight budget to clothe, clean and cook for nine kids while pregnant with her tenth. Elsewhere, mum Jo manages the challenge of getting all her 10 kids up in time for the school run before a day of housework

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Documentary using eyewitness testimony and footage from camcorders, CCTV and phones to examine traumatic, shocking, humorous and occasionally bizarre experiences of people who have become involved in disputes with neighbours. The programme also reveals how long-fought battles result in Asbos, evictions and sometimes violence

programme.title: The Mega Council Estate Next Door
programme.desc: The very different lives of people on either side of the economic divide in Islington, where the rich and the poor live shoulder to shoulder. Characters include 'Dave Elvis', who is a legend on the estate and spends all day, every day dressed as the King of Rock n' Roll, and Peter, an author who used to work for the European Parliament, but now devotes his life to writing exciting novels - if only he could get them published

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Christian and Mandy, the organisers of the annual Pride festival, are 4,000 euros short of the 18,000 required to stage the weekend-long party - and if that were not bad enough, the costumes are seven sizes too big. With two weeks left, can the duo save the day? Cameras also follow bar owner Cath as she prepares to host a hen party for 14 excited Northern Irish women and Leeds lad Peter, who publishes a free pocket guide to the Costa del Sol

programme.title: Johnny Depp: Where Did it All Go Wrong?

programme.title: Ultimate Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The documentary examines examples of officers on the front line who face abuse in the line of duty, whether that be physical, verbal and even being spat on. In Co Durham, Andy is on the hunt for a violent suspect who has stolen a car, and, on the outskirts of the suspect's home town, he finds the vehicle wrapped around a tree. Andy then heads to the suspect's house, where the team nick him and an accomplice

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red

programme.title: Suffolk Strangler: 30 Days of Terror
programme.desc: The crimes of Steven Wright, also known as the Suffolk Strangler, who killed five female sex workers in Ipswich during 2006, and was sentenced to life imprisonment in February 2008. This programme tells the tale of his crimes, as well as the steps that have been put in place nationally to try to educate both sex workers and police, in an effort to minimise the risk of this type of crime occurring again

programme.title: Missing or Murdered?
programme.desc: A look at the case of 35-year-old chef Claudia Lawrence, who disappeared in 2009 sparking one of the most high profile missing persons cases of recent years. The programme examines how media coverage of Claudia's private life impacted on the police investigation, and analyses theories of what really happened, including a recent possible sighting of her in Amsterdam and her mother's fears of sex trafficking

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Documentary using eyewitness testimony and footage from camcorders, CCTV and phones to examine the experiences of people who have become involved in disputes with neighbours. Here cameras follow the case of Northern Ireland pensioner Maire Rankin, who was murdered in Newry on Christmas Day in 2008

programme.title: Eamonn & Ruth: How the Other Half Lives
programme.desc: Poker pro Liv Boeree explains how winning or losing millions can come down to keeping one's nerve in the most fleeting of moments and Moshi Monsters creator Michael Acton Smith, who has sold more than 200 million trading cards, reveals why he is not afraid to risk failure to discover the next big thing. Ruth also talks to angel investor Lauren Maillian and Eamonn meets city stockbroker Andrew Craig to learn about the arcane workings of the world of high finance

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Bridges
programme.desc: Engineer Rob Bell sets out on a journey to discover how six of Britain's most stunning bridges were designed and built. Completed in 1890, the Forth Rail Bridge now carries around 200 trains every day. The last of the great Victorian engineering triumphs, it has grown to symbolise Scotland as much as Edinburgh Castle, bagpipes or kilts. But, as Rob learns, this incredible engineering achievement was born from the tragedy of the 1879 Tay Bridge disaster, and 73 deaths were connected with its construction and immediate aftermath

programme.title: Wildlife SOS
programme.desc: The work of volunteers treating sick, injured and orphaned animals at a wildlife sanctuary

programme.title: Divine Designs
programme.desc: Paul Binski, Professor of the History of Medieval Art at Cambridge University, visits Chelsea's Gothic-revival Holy Trinity Church designed by John Dando Sedding, and William Lethaby's thatched All Saints' Church in Brockhampton, Herefordshire, which boasts fittings by William Morris and Edward Burne-Jones of the Arts and Crafts movement

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Interior design expert Ann Maurice visits Stepney in London's East End, where she advises part-time DJ Simon Denny on how to make his two-bedroom split-level converted brewery lodge more attractive to potential buyers

programme.title: Baggage Claim (2013)
programme.desc: An air hostess is horrified at the thought of her younger sister getting married while she is still single. She realises her job gives her access to eligible men from across America, and begins clocking up thousands of air miles as she scours the country for a potential fiance. Romantic comedy, with Paula Patton and Taye Diggs

programme.title: The Spiderwick Chronicles (2008)
programme.desc: Life for identical twins Jared and Simon and their sister Mallory begins afresh when their mother inherits a spooky mansion. But after moving in, strange things begin to happen, and as the youngsters investigate, they discover a book that opens up a mysterious world of amazing supernatural creatures. Fantasy adventure, starring Freddie Highmore in a dual role alongside Sarah Bolger and Nick Nolte

programme.title: Carry On Cabby (1963)
programme.desc: The neglected wife of a workaholic taxi owner grows increasingly tired of his lack of interest in their marriage. In a ploy to bring him to his senses, she secretly launches a rival cab firm with a driver roster made up of attractive women, who soon tempt his customers away. Comedy, starring Sid James, Hattie Jacques, Kenneth Connor and Charles Hawtrey

programme.title: Hail, Caesar! (2016)
programme.desc: An executive in 1950s Hollywood is employed to bury scandals, solve problems and ensure everything goes to plan for the studio. In one fraught day, he has to contend with the kidnapping of the leading man from the set of an ancient Roman epic, a musical star's secret pregnancy, and a director who objects to having a miscast actor forced on him. The Coen brothers' comedy, starring Josh Brolin, George Clooney, Scarlett Johansson and Ralph Fiennes

programme.title: The Monuments Men (2014)
programme.desc: Seven experts in fine art are assembled into a military platoon during the Second World War, and given an unusual mission. They are sent into occupied Europe to recover priceless works of art that have fallen into Nazi hands and ensure the masterpieces survive the conflict. Fact-based drama, directed by and starring George Clooney. With Matt Damon, Bill Murray and Cate Blanchett

programme.title: The Martian (2015)
programme.desc: A manned mission to Mars is abruptly abandoned, with one crew member left for dead. However, the astronaut has survived and faces a series of challenges - to contact Earth and arrange his rescue and to survive for the years it will take for a spacecraft to reach him on resources intended to last the crew a month. Ridley Scott's sci-fi drama based on the novel by Andy Weir, starring Matt Damon, Jessica Chastain, Kristen Wiig and Chiwetel Ejiofor

programme.title: Solace (2015)
programme.desc: A psychic is recruited by the FBI to help them track down an elusive serial killer before he can strike again. The case takes a psychologically dangerous turn when he realises that the murderer he is searching for has mental powers of his own. Crime thriller, starring Anthony Hopkins, Colin Farrell and Jeffrey Dean Morgan

programme.title: Devil's Due (2014)
programme.desc: A newlywed couple are surprised to discover they are expecting their first child soon after their honeymoon. The husband decides to document his wife's pregnancy on film, but witnesses her undergoing a strange transformation that leaves him wondering if there is something supernatural about the unborn baby. Horror, starring Allison Miller and Zach Gilford

programme.title: The Rugrats Movie (1998)
programme.desc: Tommy tries to return his new brother to the baby shop, helped by Angelica, Chuckie, Phil and Lil - but the friends become lost in dense woodlands en route, turning their expedition into a scary and exciting journey of discovery. Animated comedy based on the TV series, with the voices of Elizabeth Daily, Christine Cavanaugh, Kath Soucie, Whoopi Goldberg and Busta Rhymes

programme.title: The Boxtrolls (2014)
programme.desc: The people of a small town live in fear of underground trolls, who they do not realise are actually harmless. A boy raised by the creatures must convince the townsfolk there is nothing to fear when a team of pest exterminators is hired to wipe out the friendly monsters. Stop-motion animated fantasy, with the voices of Isaac Hempstead Wright, Elle Fanning and Ben Kingsley

programme.title: The Choice (2016)
programme.desc: Two neighbours find themselves overwhelmingly attracted to each other, and make substantial changes in their lives to be together - one breaking off a long-term relationship and the other conquering his fear of commitment. Over the next 10 years, their feelings for each other are tested by a series of challenges. Romantic drama based on Nicholas Sparks' novel, starring Benjamin Walker and Teresa Palmer

programme.title: The Secret Life of Walter Mitty (2013)
programme.desc: An ordinary man seeks solace from his mundane life by drifting into daydreams where he is a heroic figure. When he and a friend are faced with unemployment, he is propelled into a globetrotting adventure in real life. Comedy drama based on James Thurber's short story, directed by and starring Ben Stiller. With Kristen Wiig, Adam Scott and Shirley MacLaine

programme.title: Suffragette Interview Special
programme.desc: Actress Anne-Marie Duff, writer Abi Morgan and director Sarah Gavron talk about the political drama

programme.title: Fantastic Four (2015)
programme.desc: A gifted young scientist joins a secret project researching inter-dimensional travel, and they return from a journey to another world having been granted superhuman powers. Their abilities make them the only hope for Earth's survival when a colleague who was lost on their expedition returns to wreak vengeance. Superhero adventure, starring Miles Teller, Michael B Jordan, Kate Mara and Jamie Bell

programme.title: American Made (2017)
programme.desc: A pilot is recruited to act as a courier for the CIA in the 1980s, carrying out covert deliveries from the US to General Noriega's regime in Panama. However, he exploits the opportunity to set up a profitable sideline trafficking drugs for a cartel. His dangerous double life takes a darker turn when he is ordered to supply weapons to far-right rebel groups operating in Nicaragua. Fact-based comedy drama, starring Tom Cruise and Domhnall Gleeson

programme.title: East Is East (1999)
programme.desc: Pakistani chip shop owner George Khan tries to keep his seven unruly children under control in 1970s Salford. Revelling in the pleasures of adolescence, the flares-wearing youngsters seem determined to ignore his moral advice - but a shock is in store for two of his teenage sons when the shadow of arranged marriages threatens their freewheeling existence. Comedy drama, starring Om Puri, Linda Bassett, Jimi Mistry and Archie Panjabi

programme.title: Cosmos (2015)
programme.desc: A student and his friend experience bizarre and disturbing events during their stay at a family-run boarding house. Thriller, starring Jonathan Genet, Johan Libereau and Sabine Azema. In French

programme.title: BSL Zone
programme.desc: Four grumpy old Deaf men travel to Blackpool's annual Deaf event hoping to make new friends. When they meet three young Deaf men, things don't quite go as planned

programme.title: Esther Waters (1948)
programme.desc: A kitchen maid succumbs to the advances of a caddish stable lad and falls pregnant - but is abandoned for another woman. Period drama, starring Dirk Bogarde and Kathleen Ryan

programme.title: Sword of Sherwood Forest (1960)
programme.desc: Legendary outlaw hero Robin Hood rallies his merry men to thwart a dastardly plan by the Sheriff of Nottingham to murder the Archbishop of Canterbury, who has denounced the sheriff's plot to confiscate another nobleman's land. Medieval adventure, starring Richard Greene, Peter Cushing, Niall MacGinnis, Richard Pasco, Sarah Branch and Oliver Reed

programme.title: The Weaker Sex (1948)
programme.desc: A housewife regrets not doing more to help the war effort after the ship her son is serving on is damaged in the D-Day Landings. Drama, starring Ursula Jeans and Thora Hird

programme.title: Alvarez Kelly (1966)
programme.desc: A cynical, mercenary cattle drover sets out to deliver a herd to soldiers at a Union base during the American Civil War. During the journey, he is kidnapped by a Confederate colonel, who hopes to persuade him to help steal the animals and take them deep into rebel territory. The Peruvian-themed feast is followed by a rendition of the country's national anthem and a traditional dance involving a candle and a donkey's jaw

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the fourth night of the competition in Winchester, Georgia Groves plans a black-tie evening complete with a red carpet, posh frocks and a spray-tan booth. It appears to be going well until Patricia Pearson reveals what she thinks of Ptol Slattery and his wine antics

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Estate agent William Tilsley throws one last dinner party as a week of competitions in Winchester, Hampshire, draws to a close. The host plans to offer his guests a vegetarian menu, with a blind food-tasting game providing the evening's entertainment. As the night draws to a close, one of the competitors rises above the rest to become the winner

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to Anglesey, where best pals Richard Tuke and Susan Hatch hope to show that Blackthorn Farm B&B, which they own, is better than any of their guests' establishments. However, they find that their close friendship is scrutinised even more than their property

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The White Swan Hotel in Yeadon, West Yorkshire, is the second stop of the competition, and owners Graham and Barbara Yellow hope their basic facilities and affordable prices will please their guests. Spiritual couple Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor get good vibes from their room, Rachel Lonergan views the hosts in a new light and breakfast is well received by the contestants, despite having to pay a supplement fee

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Vegetarians Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor host at the Old Mill House B&B in Bridport, Dorset, and both come under attack at dinner when Malcolm voices his opinions on meat eating. In the morning, a meditation session gets a mixed response, and most of the guests are left cold by the vegetarian buffet breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Rachel and Dan Lonergan welcome their guests to the Brighton Inn, East Sussex, where they hope to offer a luxury, boutique experience. Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor give their room a thorough inspection, before the groups learns the art of building a human pyramid in the afternoon's activity. At breakfast, they discover a lack of vegetarian alternatives, and the hosts suspect game-playing when they are shocked by some negative feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners come together to confront their rivals and find out how much they have been paid. Richard and Susan are keen to tackle the bathroom issues of Barbara and Graham, who bask in the comments from Malcolm and Fiona. The Old Mill House proprietors defend their vegetarian breakfast routine, but serve up snobby feedback to Rachel and Dan from the Brighton Inn

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Michael Butcher and Phil Palmer want to bring a touch of urban glamour to their new home at Christmas Farm near Newbury, Berkshire, by creating a modern farmhouse that combines an open-plan party pad with functional spaces below. As the builders set to work on the handcrafted flint exterior, complete with a vast trapezoid window, the pair juggle construction with farming the land, and in an attempt to make the project work financially, they set up a microbrewery in the barn. Kevin McCloud follows their progress

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Registrar Ling is concerned about 23-year-old David, who has been brought to King's College Hospital after having seizures for 30 minutes and isn't responding to drugs. Ling weighs up the benefits of putting him into an induced coma to stop the attacks. Meanwhile, 49-year-old Suzette is brought in following an accident involving a cyclist, and Selin is accompanied by her best friend Maria to receive treatment for a cut

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The team treats 78-year-old Kevin, who suffered a cardiac arrest at the gym and fortunately received life-saving treatment at the scene from an instructor trained to use a portable defibrillator. Meanwhile, 23-year-old paramedic Declan is sent for an emergency CT scan, and Monica, 83, is experiencing numbness in her leg following a fall at home, and her initial X-ray appears to indicate she has a broken neck

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Bishop Brennan orders Ted and Dougal to campaign against a controversial film - but as a result of their protest it becomes the biggest box-office hit in Craggy Island's history. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon, Frank Kelly and Pauline McLynn

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The attractive author of a steamy novel comes to stay on Craggy Island - and it is not long before Ted mistakenly jumps to the conclusion that she has fallen for him. Guest starring Gemma Craven

programme.desc: Tim Shaw and mechanic Fuzz Townshend go to Leeds to repair an AC Aceca, a British sports car that has been kept and loved by its owner Monet since the early 1970s

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and mechanic Fuzz Townshend head to West Sussex to rescue an iconic 1970s motor - and the first truly successful Japanese car export - the mighty Datsun 240z

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and mechanic Fuzz Townshend head to Croydon, south London, to rescue a star car that has been wallowing in its own garage for over 30 years. Its owner, Peter, is a serial petrolhead who has been fixing up motors for friends and family for most of his life, putting his own classic vehicle on the back burner. It's a 1964 Volvo P1800S, one of the most stylish cars to come off any production line, and the vehicle synonymous with cult 1960s TV series The Saint

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Clairvoyant Yvette Tamara hosts the first dinner party in north Kent, and the night gets off to a spooky start when data analyst John Tower and working mother Yemi Christopher-Soares discover a ghost-busting machine. Meanwhile, jeweller Lisa Townend is so impressed with Yvette's abilities that she wants to return for a psychic reading

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Data analyst John Tower hosts the second dinner party in north Kent, and lets his passion for military re-enactment show when he barks orders at the table. However, not all the guests enjoy his fatty pork stew and psychedelic dessert, and two argue over dating etiquette

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Project manager Yemi Christopher-Soares hosts the third dinner party in north Kent, where she hopes the guests will enjoy her Nigerian-inspired menu and appreciate her love of all things spicy

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Jono Simm hosts the penultimate dinner party in north Kent and is convinced his wholesome and hearty menu is the way to win. However, two of his guests are less than impressed by his cooking

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Party-loving Lisa Townend hosts the final dinner party in north Kent, and hopes that a menu full of French fancies and innovative entertainment in the form of human roulette, will be enough to impress her guests and see her crowned winner of the £1,000 cash prize

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to Charing, Kent, where landlady Natalie Palmer hopes to show that her B&B, The Oak, is better than any of her guests' establishments. Everything starts off well when a couple are delighted to have enough room for their yoga mats, but the rest of the party are unimpressed with the extractor fans

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants visit the White Lady in Worstead, Norfolk, on their second night, where proprietor Dennis Gilligan tells his guests about a spooky presence that resides there. The guests get competitive in a round of clay shooting, and debate whether they believe in the existence of ghosts

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to Cranleigh House B&B in Combe Martin, Devon, where former couple Katherine Armitage and Stewart Johnston offer alternative accommodation for those who enjoy yoga and are seeking spiritual enlightenment. They hope the group will send out energy for world peace and healing, but Mike and Denise question whether the whole thing is a joke

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mike and Denise Mitchell welcome the contestants to Beaches B&B in Paignton, Devon, which only opened for business a few weeks ago. Fellow competitor Katherine puts the owners through their paces at breakfast when she asks for something off the menu, and the hosts are pleasantly surprised at feedback time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners come together for one last time to discover what they have been paid, and the identity of the winner. The Oak owner Natalie wants to know why some of her guests were not enthused by her extractor fans, but she herself receives aggression from Dennis over her criticism of the White Lady. Katherine and Stuart defend their vegetarian breakfast, and Mike and Denise deal with negative comments about their cereal selection

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Oil and gas co-ordinator Danielle Greenwood is keen to impress as she hosts the first dinner party in the Merseyside area, as she welcomes divorcee Jade Chantelle Short, Michael Buble impersonator Jamie Somers, pastoral care manager Steven Booth, and nursery school teacher Anna Senior into her home. Danielle takes no risks with a down-to-earth menu, which she hopes will help bag her the £1,000 prize at the end of the week

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Pastoral care manager Steven Booth hosts the second Merseyside dinner party, and names each course after a Tina Turner song, hoping to win the £1,000 prize. The food is well received, but tensions rise when one guest makes a tactless comment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Glamorous PR representative Jade Chantelle hosts the third night in Merseyside, where she hopes a casino-themed evening with lashings of champagne and a high-class menu will help her to win the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Primary school teacher Anna Senior hosts the fourth night in Merseyside with a fairy tale-themed evening. Her menu consists of a Mad Hatter's soup and sandwich, a special pork dish cooked three ways and baked cheesecake for dessert, but she must try to control the tension between Jamie and Jade to have a successful party

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Michael Buble impersonator Jamie Somers hosts the final Merseyside dinner party, but may have to put aside his differences with guest Jade if he is to be crowned the winner of the £1,000 cash prize

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: In 2003 Gil and Hillary Briffa decided to retire to southern Spain, where they planned to build a contemporary home in the Andalusian hills against the backdrop of more traditional properties. A few years later, Gil died, but now Kevin McCloud revisits Hillary to find out whether her residence has settled into its landscape and how life is treating her as a British widow living abroad

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics brave harsh winter weather to respond to emergency calls, including a driver who crashed on the icy M6 and a woman in police custody who keeps losing consciousness

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Police and paramedics in Cheshire respond to more incidents, including a 20-year-old who has returned home after a night out, collapsed in front of her parents and stopped breathing. Plus, a man calls the emergency services to say that a naked stranger his mother met at a punk gig has fallen down their stairs

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Four-year-old Alfie rushed in with a broken thighbone after falling off his bike, the medical team suspects 19-year-old rugby player Chris has suffered neck and spinal injuries, while James, 42, arrives with chest pains and breathing difficulties. Documentary following life inside one of Britain's busiest A&E departments at King's College Hospital in south London.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the conclusion of Gordon Ramsay's visit to La Galleria 33 in Boston, Massachusetts, he confronts sisters Rita and Lisa with an ultimatum, and finds himself stuck in a war between the waiters, chefs and owners over who is to blame for the Italian restaurant's failings

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: For 10 years, publicans Chris and Sue from Shropshire have fancied owning their dream holiday home, and now they go in search for it on Portugal's stunning Silver Coast, with £100,000 to invest

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Max McMurdo and Henry Cole visit a motorbike fan, making table lamps from engines and turning a pallet and iron wheels into a stylish modern coffee table

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head east to Newark in search of their latest upcycling prospects. Simon finds an antique European threshing board, which he turns into a coffee table, and Henry's all at sea with a ship's porthole

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie finishes her mother Isobel's apartment. Painted and decorated, it now has her fathers framed pictures on the walls, a stunning 200-year-old mahogany canopy, plus furniture. Elsewhere, Jayne and Steve finish the Oriental Room and are ready to take in guests

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Kevin and Liz Davies, who plan to build a five-bedroom home complete with a swimming pool in north Somerset. They are expecting to spend around £610,000, but realise they must rethink the sink with a view and woodburner when the presenter casts his eye over the designs

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Owner Alfie Bubbles and former Brookside star Philip Olivier welcome guests to a Beatles-themed hotel - The Yellow Submarine - in Liverpool. However, one guest is unimpressed when she falls ill on a sea voyage, and breakfast turns into a comedy of errors

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Megan Edgcumbe and Dick Stanley host the latest round of the competition at Manor Lodge in Cornwall. Things get off to a good start when the guests find their rooms clean, but later the hostess reveals her upper-class background and a Cumberland sausage causes a stir at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: James and Judith Calderbank invite their fellow competitors to the rural Ashculme House in Devon for the third visit of the week, but the couple's back-to-basics approach does not go down well with all the guests. Workaholic Alfie worries that having no phone signal will affect his business, while competitive Megan and Dick relish finding dust in the outside building

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competitors have great expectations of Kevin Wainwright and partner David Little's Satis House in Suffolk, but the last visit of the week sees old resentments resurface as Megan and Dick eagerly point out faults in the hosts' presentation - including an oversight in their room and overly crispy bacon for breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. Alfie and rival owner Megan engage in a debate about breakfasts and heating, while David and Kevin become a little emotional before the week's winner is announced

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Pregnant rhino Asha goes into labour overnight, meaning there is a surprise waiting for the keepers when they arrive to work the next morning. Meanwhile, Malayan tapirs Margery and Betong say goodbye to their daughter, Solo, who is leaving to start a new life at a zoo in Germany, and female dead leaf mantis Minnie is looking for her first partner

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer are in Northamptonshire with Isabel and Louis Prat, whose three decades of marital bliss is being shaken by their house. The former social worker and nurse are desperate to enjoy their retirement in their dream home, but while Louis is passionate about the abode and the local area, Isabel hates their property. With a refurb budget of £70,000, Kirstie is confident she can give Isabel the ground floor space she wants, while Phil takes to the road with the couple to show them the amazing homes they could buy

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: A couple want to turn their 20-room chateau into a B&B and yoga retreat, while two musicians start renovating their final untouched room - the old library

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets actor Sean Simons, who has bought the ruins of Cloontykilla Castle in Co Roscommon with a view to using them to create a spectacular mock 16th-century home. However, the outlandish plans - including Jacuzzis in the battlements and water-spouting gargoyles - have Kevin worried the project could become a garish nightmare, and his scepticism increases when he realises Sean has not consulted an architect. Disaster seems to be looming as the contractors grow frustrated and planning regulations are ignored, but somehow things slowly begin to take shape - until a national economic crisis plunges everything into chaos

programme.title: Inside the Zoo: Down Under
programme.desc: Keepers hope to mate two of the sea lions, an olive python is treated for chronic back pain, and a giant aldabra tortoise celebrates its 103rd birthday

programme.title: It Was Alright In
programme.desc: Matt Lucas narrates a look back at the decade's TV shows, meeting people who appeared in them, those who watched them and their creators. The first of two programmes focuses on pleasure and leisure on 1970s television, with featured clips including outrageous adult jokes in pre-watershed sitcoms, smoking cigarettes on prime-time panel shows, a surprising history lesson aimed at children, and St Bernard dogs being offered for prizes. The footage is also seen through the eyes of people who missed the decade altogether and who find the moral values exhibited to be a real eye-opener

programme.title: Sex Toy Stories
programme.desc: Documentary following eight volunteers working with the Ann Summers company to create a new vibrator. The contributors, including a 69-year-old grandmother, a twentysomething virgin and a middle-aged divorcee who has never achieved an orgasm, test existing products on the market and find inspiration in their kitchen cupboards for their dream sex toy, hoping the retailer can get it on to shop shelves in time for Valentine's Day

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and-numbers quiz, with captain Jon Richardson joined by Sara Pascoe to take on his opposite number Sean Lock, assisted by Vic Reeves. programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits the home of New Nordic cuisine - Copenhagen - where he goes foraging for rosehip and learns about the art of pickling buds, bark and flowers. He's cheered to find plenty of traditional favourites, such as the city's famous Smorrebrod open sandwiches on rye bread, delicious poached turbot with cabbage and lemon butter sauce and - of course - the ubiquitous Danish pastry. Back at home, Rick cooks fried pork belly with lovage potatoes and parsley sauce, which has been voted Denmark's favourite dish

Plus, Jeremy grabs the keys to two variants of the Mercedes-Benz SLS AMG supercar - the V8-engined Black Series with a roaring 622bhp, and the 740bhp Electric Drive, which is powered by four electric motors.
