programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: Herbert Rowse Armstrong, who was convicted of murdering his wife with arsenic. Much of the evidence against him was circumstantial and the scientific testimony remains disputed. This episode examines both sides of the case and asks whether the British justice system made a terrible mistake

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Enola Gay drops the first atomic bomb on Hiroshima in August 1945 as America bids to secure an unconditional surrender from the Japanese, even though conventional bombing raids had already flattened the country. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Series exploring ruined engineering projects that have been put to alternative use, including the site of the Chernobyl disaster and a Nazi holiday complex

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A town in West Virginia that fell victim to the pace of change when steam trains became obsolete and an underground complex in Italy that had been abandoned for nearly 2,000 years

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey from Derby to Lindisfarne, with this leg beginning in Boston, south Lincolnshire, where he explores the connection between the town and its American namesake. At Southwell, Nottinghamshire, he discovers the origins of a favourite Victorian apple before heading into West Yorkshire to visit an imposing institution built in Menston to provide asylum for those with mental health issues. In nearby Wakefield, Michael boards one of Britain's least frequent services, before heading to Hensall, North Yorkshire

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins this leg of his journey in the East Yorkshire town of Hessle, where he learns about the technology that made the Humber Bridge possible. In nearby Hull, Michael hears from local MP Alan Johnson about anti-slavery campaigner William Wilberforce, before travelling to Scarborough Castle, where the founder of the Quaker movement was once imprisoned. His last stop of the day is York, where he learns what made the city a centre for the sweet-making industry

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Lyme Park in Cheshire, famously the location for the 1995 TV adaptation of Pride and Prejudice. Items brought in to be valued include a pair of pistols used to protect royal mail coaches from highwaymen, a tea caddy disguised as a pile of books, and a portrait of actress Sarah Bernhardt that once belonged to Elton John

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians explore the life of the South Devon branch line before the Beeching cuts of the 1960s. Peter Ginn joins a footplate crew and receives coal-shovelling tips from a veteran steam engine driver of 63 years' experience, and with Alex he learns about one of Scotland's most lucrative exports. Ruth Goodman finds out about the dangers involved in working on the Victorian railways, and how those who lost limbs were catered for by a dedicated prosthetics workshop. She also discovers how the railways came to the rescue when a deadly disease wiped out almost the entire stock of London cattle

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Heavy haulage experts Ian Bagladi and Jamie Turnbull need to move a classical but abandoned 63-year-old, 70-tonne locomotive, through the English countryside

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A ruined compound on the edge of a peninsula in Colombia which was once home to a famous outlaw and the strange towers looming over red-tinted waters in a Portuguese landscape

programme.title: Wartime Crime
programme.desc: The stories of two murderers in London and Berlin, who both took advantage of the Blackouts during the Second World War to commit their crimes undetected

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The award-winning documentary series reveals how the end of the Second World War resulted in Britain's military and economic exhaustion, leaving Europe at the mercy of sparring superpowers as the Cold War era dawned. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins the fourth leg of his journey from Derby to Lindisfarne in Middlesbrough, visiting one of the last iron foundries in the town and helping cast a carrot valve for a steam engine. His next stop is Darlington, Co Durham, where he meets the editor of the Northern Echo and finds out about the colourful history of WT Stead, one of his predecessors. Michael then heads to St Paul's monastery in Jarrow, Tyne and Wear, to learn about eighth-century monk the Venerable Bede, known as 'the father of English history', before finishing in Hexham, Northumberland, where he visits a historic ginger beer emporium

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off from Newcastle on the final leg of his journey from Derby to Lindisfarne, finding out about the world's earliest swing bridge and its inventor William Armstrong. He also travels by boat from the port of Seahouses to examine the story of lighthouse keeper's daughter Grace Darling, who, along with her father, rescued nine people from tumultuous seas in 1838. On the Holy Island of Lindisfarne, Michael explores the lime kilns and finds out how Christianity spread from here across northern England in the 7th century

programme.title: Trains That Changed the World
programme.desc: The railroads ended slavery in the USA, but trains can be agents of oppression too. The British Empire built railways to rule the world, enabling it to control the empire

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A classic car fan with a love of the Jaguar E-Type reluctantly sends his dream car to auction, hoping to buy a motorhome with the money. Two Ford Escorts get Dave's pulse racing

For the signature challenge, they must prepare a batch of classic eclairs, while the technical round will test their skills with puff pastry. Finally, for the showstopper challenge, they must immortalise their greatest fear in cake form

programme.title: Jon Richardson: Old Man Tour
programme.desc: A chance to see a live performance recorded at Blackpool's historic Grand Theatre, on what was the final night of the comedian's sell-out, 158-date UK and Ireland tour. Here, Jon takes to the stage to comment - and generally complain - on topics including parenthood, Brexit and haircuts. The question of why nobody but him can correctly load a dishwasher is also raised. Alas, Jon can offer no solutions to all the problems he identifies, but nevertheless provides plenty of mirth as he fails to reach a viable conclusion to his grouses

programme.title: First Dates

Meanwhile, 86-year-old Douglas and 76-year-old Joan bond over a shared love of musicals, and social worker Becky 36, meets blockbuster movie safety officer Dave during her second visit

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: This edition focuses on the policing of roads in Northamptonshire, which has borne more than its fair share of funding cuts. This has resulted in fewer police officers available to attend a huge range of traffic issues, from the routine to the surreal, on the county's 3,000-mile road network, as well as a spike in crime

programme.title: Me and My Penis
programme.desc: Documentary in which men talk openly about their penis and how it feels to be a man, while artist Ajamu makes intimate photographic portraits based on the sitters' experiences to help them rethink the way they see their bodies and themselves. The men talk about the pleasures and physical realities of the penis, including sex, masturbation and erections, but also tell stories of infertility, violence, sexual abuse, homophobia and mental health, questioning taboos and the definitions of masculinity

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Essex is the location for three couples to host a trio of dinner parties, including a pantomime-themed menu and an evening of north Indian cuisine, while a couple's joke at one guest's expense threaten to cost them valuable points?

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Belfast, three couples compete to host the best dinner party. Sarah and Paddy kick things off with a fine-dining feast for guests Louise and Danny and Emma and Samson. The hosts mix Eastern and traditional fare. Then, on night two Emma and Samson treat their guests to some Mozambican delights. After a first course that whistles and wows, palates need to be cleansed for the main course, but a chilly chat about ice cream leaves Louise red in the face. On the final night, Louise and Danny confound everyone's expectations with a sailing-based starter. A spot of model behaviour excites the guests, and a mountain of meringue impresses, but is it enough to win the £1,000 prize?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Baltimore, Maryland, where Denise Whiting has alienated customers by trademarking the word 'hon', a term of endearment used by the local community. When she opened Cafe Hon in 1992 it was a roaring success, but as a result of Denise's unwavering business ambition, people started to boycott the restaurant, leaving her frustrated by falling sales

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay's next stop is Philadelphia, where family run restaurant Chiarella's is struggling to survive in an area full of thriving Italian eateries. Tommy and Dina Defino's marriage is on the verge of collapse and his sense of personal failure only adds to the troubles of their crisis-ridden business

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three previous restaurants he tried to save, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he heads for West Babylon, New York, where he helped the inexperienced owners of restaurant Classic American deal with their escalating debts, before returning to Davide in Boston, an Italian restaurant run by two warring brothers. Finally, he visits Down City, a restaurant in Providence, Rhode Island, run by hot-headed owner Abby

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to New Jersey's Spanish Pavilion, which is run by two warring brothers whose staff spend more time using their mobile phones than working. The chef plans to turn the eatery's fortunes around, and starts by giving the dining room and menu a modern makeover

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet the Olliers from Stenhousemuir in Scotland, where husband and wife Steven and Mhairi are undecided whether to sell their home or renovate it. Kirstie produces a plan that revolutionises the whole house, while Phil is tasked to find them a home that's a short throw away from their beloved cricket club

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and-numbers quiz, with Lee Mack and Victoria Coren Mitchell taking on Alan Carr and Dane Baptiste. Sam Simmons joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: A compilation of the best bits from recent series with host Jimmy Carr, team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson, and Countdown regulars Susie Dent and Rachel Riley, joined by a host of top comedians including Alan Carr, Richard Ayoade, Roisin Conaty, Aisling Bea, Nick Mohammed, Joe Wilkinson, Sara Pascoe, Kevin Bridges and Joe Lycett

programme.title: George Clarke's National Trust Unlocked
programme.desc: George heads for Northumberland, where he takes in one of the most modern Victorian houses in existence - the water-powered, cliff-top mansion Cragside. He also adds his name to the list of dukes, earls and viscounts who've made the astonishing Grade I-listed Cliveden Gardens their playground over the past 300 years, and investigates the unique home of a fellow architect at Snowshill Manor in the Cotswolds, a treasure trove of more than 22,000 curiosities collected from all around the world

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Return of the documentary following life inside an A&E department. The series begins with the story of 22-year-old police officer Hazel, who is rushed to St George's after crashing her motorbike while out on a ride with her father. Meanwhile, 86-year-old John is admitted to A&E with a suspected 'triple A' - a ruptured aortic aneurysm in his abdomen, a condition that can be fatal. The doctors must work quickly to diagnose the cause of the internal bleeding, but their investigations reveal further complications. John's children share the story of their father's life, and his surprising secret that sent shockwaves through the family

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: The team looks back at snack-based stories from past episodes. Jimmy Doherty investigates unusual shaped crisps, Matt Tebbutt discovers the dangers of double-dipping tortilla chips, Helen Lawal reports on whether rice cakes are as good a diet snack as commonly believed, and Kate Quilton delves into the world of macadamia nuts in Hawaii, and uncovers the reasons for rising numbers of children with peanut allergies

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Jamaican lifeguard Lenny Irving serves up Caribbean cuisine at the second of the week's dinner parties in Belfast. He also hopes to win over his guests by complementing his menu with strong rum - and seems particularly keen to impress Merchant Navy officer Fiona Rush

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Freelance feminist writer Cat Anderson hosts the third dinner party in Belfast, and hopes to win over her guests with her bohemian chic style and live music. However, her efforts are wasted on lifeguard Lenny Irving, who is only interested in Fiona Rush

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Merchant Navy officer Fiona Rush hosts the final dinner party in Belfast, and hopes to impress with a nautical-themed evening. However, one of her guests is unimpressed with the amount of fish on the menu, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Newly-weds Karen and John Leonard from Essex are about to retire. With the last of the six boys they have between them about to leave home, they're looking for a new adventure and a complete change of lifestyle. Scarlette Douglas shows them five properties in the sunny south of Gran Canaria for their budget of £175,000

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Meudon Hotel in Falmouth, Cornwall, where owner Gaye Woods hopes to impress rivals Tim and Mikey, Joan and Geoff, and Sue and Chris

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The team heads to Sue's house in Tiverton, Devon, finding a moped that brings back memories, and fighting over a petrol gauge that Simon O'Brien wants to upcycle into a clock

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Double-length episode. Donny issues a lawsuit against Daphne for jilting him at the altar, and Mel plots revenge against Niles after he abandoned his marriage to her. Comedy, starring David Hyde Pierce, Jane Leeves and Saul Rubinek

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to New Jersey's Spanish Pavilion, which is run by two warring brothers whose staff spend more time using their mobile phones than working. The chef plans to turn the eatery's fortunes around, and starts by giving the dining room and menu a modern makeover

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits four restaurants he earlier tried to save, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or fell by the wayside. First he visits the Spanish Pavillion in New Jersey, then heads to California to see the owners of Kingston Cafe, French-themed La Frite, and Capri

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A mother-of-three wants a family friendly property in the thriving resort of Benalmádena on the Costa del Sol, with its sunny beaches and lively atmosphere. With a budget of £135,000 and specifying the need for sea views, Danni Menzies sets out to deliver the dream place

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin shows five properties in Palma, Majorca to Fred and Susan, who live and work on a farm in Norfolk and are looking for somewhere to escape to during winter

programme.title: Jimmy's Big Bee Rescue
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jimmy Doherty goes in search of the Secretary of State for Environment, in the hope of gaining government support for his plans. The programme examines how a radical transformation of the British countryside is needed to create a more bee-friendly environment, and reveals the results of the first episode's efforts to boost bee numbers in Peterborough

programme.title: Crime and Punishment
programme.desc: The documentary follows the Crown Prosecution Service and detectives from Hampshire's Major Crime team as they struggle to prosecute a man accused of killing a 13-year-old schoolgirl - a case made more difficult by budget cuts. The film also follows a Southampton detective and CPS lawyer as they team up to charge a man accused of exposing himself to a six-year-old boy

programme.title: Council House Britain
programme.desc: Documentary following frontline council workers and residents in Southwark, south London, one of the biggest housing associations in the country. Housing officer Charmain helps a man who has lost his key, deals with a window cleaner performing a high-wire act and worries about a vulnerable resident who is not answering his door. Andy from pest control tackles an empty flat with a fridge full of cockroaches

programme.title: The Yorkshire Jobcentre
programme.desc: More from life behind the scenes at a busy public employment service. Penny used to run a multimillion pound retail chain in Nigeria with her husband, but since their divorce the 49-year-old has found herself living in Leeds on universal credit. Lindsey in the social justice team helps Dean prepare for a test which, if he passes, could lead to a job in the construction industry, while Afghan asylum seeker Zaref not only does needs a job, but has to earn more than £18,600 to bring his wife to the U

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: More of the most memorable moments from the past five years, including one of the zoo's most enduring love stories at Penguin Island. At 22, Rudd is the oldest penguin on the island and has been with his partner Spike for more than 15 years. Meanwhile, Silvery gibbon Alvin is keen to get close to his partner Tilu again, but ever since they had a baby together, young Eko has proven a bit of a passion killer

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at homes by the sea as they continue to celebrate two decades of the property search programme. Inyang and Gregor have sold up in Glasgow and want to move somewhere they have never even visited - Cornwall. London fashion designer Shane has his heart set on the Kent coast, young-at-heart pensioners Peggy and Jon are swapping Derby for the Norfolk coast and Jemma and Graham, who moved back from LA, are struggling to find their dream seaside home in Essex

programme.title: Lodgers for Codgers
programme.desc: With the majority of under-30s struggling to get on the property ladder and millions of over-60s sitting on empty rooms, young and old tackle Britain's housing crisis by trialling a unique solution - moving in together and seeing how they get along. In this edition, 21-year-old poet Marvell leaves his east London council house to lodge with retired couple Lynne and John in their converted chapel in the Somerset town of Glastonbury. Meanwhile, in Birmingham, 20-year-old Sophie co-habits with no-nonsense pensioner Eunice. How she will cope living under her landlord's strict house rules remains to be seen

programme.title: Inside Missguided: Made in Manchester
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes at one of Britain's biggest online fashion retailer's offices to explore what life is like for their young, female-dominated workforce at their offices in the heart of Manchester. In the final episode, cameras follow the Missguided team as they prepare for one of the most important days of the year, Black Friday. To hit their targets, the brand are launching their first ever ski wear range, and lead creative Treasure is sent to Austria for a photoshoot in sub-zero temperatures

programme.title: Supershoppers
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Sabrina Grant reveal ways to make big savings on takeaways, supermarket deliveries and eating out, with the team investigating restaurant discount cards. There is also advice on getting the most from online supermarket shopping, a look at an app that offers leftover food for a discount price, and tips on buying coffee on the high street

programme.title: Two and a Half Men

programme.title: Two and a Half Men

programme.title: Two and a Half Men

programme.title: Two and a Half Men

programme.title: Two and a Half Men

programme.desc: An evil crime lord tries to capture a boy who has supernatural powers, but a team of international police officers is on his trail. Comedy adventure, with Jackie Chan, Lee Evans, Julian Sands, Claire Forlani and John Rhys-Davies

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement agents Paul Bohill and Steve Pinner are in Birmingham on a potentially dangerous, full-scale eviction of a large traveller community. Meanwhile, in London's diamond district Brian O'Shaughnessy and Delroy Anglin are chasing nearly £6,000 owed by a jeweller to an unhappy customer, although a solicitor tries to convince Delroy his client owes nothing

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court Enforcement Agents Paul and Steve are sent to north London to evict a young Romanian mother who owes £6,000 in unpaid rent, but when they arrive, they discover a family home that is not quite what it appears to be. Meanwhile, in Kent, Brian and Alan turn up the pressure on a debtor who has admitted to owing more than £7,000, but claims he cannot afford to pay up - despite having a garage full of expensive tools. Later, in south-west London, Brian accompanies Delroy as he tries to recover a £400,000 debt from a residential property, and when the pair trace the defaulter to an upmarket cafe, the man in charge lies about his identity

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: One-off edition in which the High Court enforcement agents are caught in the crossfire of three bitter family disputes, including a debt-dodging man who pushes his panic-stricken mother over the edge. Plus, a family booted onto the streets at night by a ruthless aunt, and a man who won't engage with the agents and loses his car

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In this special episode, the High Court enforcement agents have to deal with a fraudulent tenant, an evasive debtor and a mother in denial. Steve and Ben are in west London to see a tenant who owes £14,000 in unpaid rent, but when they gain access to the property they discover the house has divided up like a hotel and is being sublet. In Sussex, Brian and Del chase a debt of over £10,000 owed to a van rental business for damaged vehicles, before heading to Kent to try to reclaim £6,000 of unpaid rent from a woman financially dependent on a new partner after a disastrous divorce

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In south London, father-and-son team Del and Dael Anglin face death threats from a debtor's father, after arriving at a property to collect a debt of over £3,500. Meanwhile, in east London, two agents are confronted with a tenant who claims to have had no prior warning that he is being evicted from his tiny bedsit. Del and Brian try to recover £6,000 owed by a London hotel to a catering supply company, and Paul and Steve must tackle a difficult eviction

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: A young mum locked out of her home by the young couple she allowed to stay there for free while the wife had a baby. Plus, an east London landlord who found half a dozen strangers living in his house, after his original tenant had run off and sublet the property. Meanwhile, Harrow Council raid a small two-bedroom flat in unsafe conditions

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Catching up with the victims and villains of the housing rental market. Rachel intended to rent out her Snowdonia cottage for only three months. Four years later her tenant is still there and not paying any rent. Plus, the stories of Stevenage landlord Derek, whose tenant Kellie proved so troublesome that the police had to be called, and Claud from south London, who was left £30,000 in debt when a professional couple split up

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The buy-to-let documentary series looks back at cases from earlier episodes to find out if matters were resolved or have only got worse. They include a Yorkshire landlord who had to call in eviction specialists to remove tenants from the house he inherited from his grandparents, and a bus driver who managed to win back possession of his home from a nightmare tenant. But just as the eviction was going through, she threatened to call the police

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: Cleaning duo Jon and Brennan take on some of Britain's filthiest and most cluttered homes. In three days, the pair bring the grubbiest homes back to life

Comedy, starring Vince Vaughn, Malin Akerman, Jon Favreau and Jason Bateman

programme.desc: The kids insist on squandering their pocket money, so Michael makes a misguided effort to teach them to manage their finances properly

programme.title: Benefits Britain: Life on the Dole
programme.desc: Documentary following the inhabitants of the seaside village of Jaywick in Essex, where more than half of the residents are on state welfare. Tony Taylor claims £51.40 a week and does odd jobs in exchange for food, enabling his benefits money to go a bit further, while Claire has moved from east London's Canning Town in search of a better life with her fiance Luke, their young baby and her two older children, who live with a host of pets in a two-bedroom house

programme.title: Benefits Britain: Life on the Dole
programme.desc: This edition focuses on young people living on state welfare, including 18-year-old Dean from Harlow in Essex, who has no intention of getting a job unless forced. Twentysomethings Danielle and her boyfriend Josh are unable to work due to anxiety and depression and have moved to Blackpool to make a new start. However, with four children between them from previous relationships and another on the way they are struggling to make ends meet on a joint benefits income of around £100 a week

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul Bohill and Steve and Ben Pinner face their biggest eviction yet - taking on squatters in a large central London office block. With the help of the police, they try to remove a suspected group of 70 from the building, but with a barricade in place, the team faces a challenging operation ahead. Elsewhere, officers are confronted by an army of family and friends when they attempt to enforce a writ at the property of a debtor, and face a difficult eviction at the north London home of a heavily pregnant tenant and her family, after the council fails to pay the rent to the landlord

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court enforcement officers Paul and Steve make an extraordinary discovery inside a locked bedroom, and immediately call for police back-up. Brian and Graham head to Swindon Town football club to recover a debt, while Paul and Ben have difficulty evicting a couple from a house in Kent after learning they have six children and nowhere else to go

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Documentary giving an insight into cash-strapped Britain from the perspectives of debtors, creditors and those who collect the payments, including High Court enforcement bailiffs and repossession teams. Paul and Ben are called in to evict squatters from a residential property, but something about the squat does not quite add up for the team

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Brian O'Shaughnessy and Graham Aldred's first case sees them in a Brighton health spa as they search for assets to seize in pursuit of a £20,000 debt. Following a stand-off, they devise a way to work with the owner and her professional advisers to come up with a solution. Later, Graham reveals his softer side when he revisits a woman who defaulted on a debt payment plan

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A suspect assaults Steve with his car keys, leaving the officer in need of urgent medical attention, while Charlie and Paul pursue a motorist who drove off from a garage without paying for fuel. Chris and Sophie spot a bunny-hopping learner driver who already has eight points on his provisional licence

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Mick leads a team raiding two flats whose occupants are suspected of producing and selling a drug known as spice. Duncan is involved in a high-speed pursuit, which ends with tracker dog Tia on the escaping offender's trail

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Australian soap opera, charting the lives and loves of the residents of the suburb of Erinsborough

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Australian soap opera charting the latest comings and goings of the residents of Summer Bay

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Enforcement officers Wayne and Dan continue their mission to identify the gang responsible for one of the biggest fly tips in Cardiff, more determined than ever to catch their prey. Environmental health officer Suzanne pays a visit to a takeaway restaurant in Worcester, and a London canal side is the scene of a battle between council enforcers and a waste-loving artist who has hoarded mountains of rubbish

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Expats Russell and Lindsay hope to attract punters to their convenience store with a selection of pastries, while retired nurse Susie and her husband Reg, who volunteer at a dog shelter, have their hands full with a littler of 10 puppies. Life coach Svetlana's online rants about the behaviour of expats on the Costa del Sol have gone viral. But as she prepares to record another monologue, she has a surprise up her sleeve

programme.title: It's Your Fault I'm Fat
programme.desc: Sixteen-year-old Jake lives in Peterborough with his mum Nikola, but weighing in at almost 20 stone, his late-night gaming and fast food habits are out of control

programme.title: How to Have a Better Orgasm
programme.desc: Seven women give intimate interviews about their diverse experience of orgasms, sharing a variety of stories and techniques covering a range of ages. Personal accounts reveal that, across the board, there's a turning point in understanding orgasms and learning how to have a better one. For Helen, it was her first experience of lesbian sex, which made her realise how important communication is to sex, while Sam attributes it to meeting her husband, because it was the first time someone cared about her pleasure

programme.title: The Sex Business
programme.desc: Documentary looking at how people buy, sell and market sex, hearing from an array of individuals working within Britain's multimillion-pound sex industry

programme.title: Britain's Poshest Brothel

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: The spirit of old Salem comes to Cabot Cove as Jessica investigates strange happenings involving a woman who has been dead for three centuries. Guest starring Roddy McDowall and Dee Wallace Stone, with Angela Lansbury

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica jets off to attend a prestigious international literary conference in Moscow, where she is soon suspected of espionage. Guest starring David McCallum, with Angela Lansbury

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A waitress is found raped and murdered outside a nightclub, with suspicion falling on her ex-boyfriend. However, the detectives get a new lead when they discover the victim was in an abusive love affair with her boss, while news of her treatment is mistakenly leaked to her father - who vows to take revenge on the attacker. Joe Morton (Law & Order) guest stars, alongside Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

Drama, starring Mariska Hargitay and Christopher Meloni

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Columbo: Ransom for a Dead Man (1971)
programme.desc: A devious lawyer hatches the perfect murder plot - she kills her husband, tells the police he has been kidnapped and leaves the body at the ransom drop, making it look like the criminals who abducted him finished him off. However, the crumpled cop sees through her ruse and begins picking apart her story in his inimitable manner. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Lee Grant and Patricia Mattick

programme.title: Columbo: A Matter of Honour (1976)
programme.desc: The sleuth's holiday in Mexico is disrupted by a bizarre death involving a suave retired bullfighter, who has killed his best friend using one of the animals as a murder weapon - but is determined his spotless image and reputation will remain untarnished. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Ricardo Montalban, A Martinez and Pedro Armendariz Jr

programme.title: Columbo: Requiem for a Falling Star (1973)
programme.desc: A vicious gossip columnist digs the dirt on an ageing film actress, who responds by taking on her most challenging role yet - as a real-life murderer. But when the actress kills her own assistant in error, she looks for a way to shift the blame. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Anne Baxter, Mel Ferrer, Kevin McCarthy and multi-Oscar-winning costume designer Edith Head in a guest appearance as herself

programme.title: Columbo: A Trace of Murder (1997)
programme.desc: A conniving woman yearns to divorce her husband and set up home with her lover - but a pre-nuptial agreement means she stands to lose out. Instead, she devises a cunning plan to nullify the contract, framing her unsuspecting spouse for the murder of a business rival. However, she reckons without the shabby sleuth's unfailing ability to uncover the truth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Shera Danese, Barry Corbin and David Rasche

programme.title: Columbo Likes the Nightlife (2003)
programme.desc: The disheveled cop is drawn into the world of the Los Angeles club scene when he investigates the apparent suicide of a tabloid reporter, and uncovers evidence of a blackmail plot. Detective drama, with Peter Falk, Matthew Rhys and Jennifer Sky

programme.title: Columbo: An Exercise in Fatality (1974)
programme.desc: The owner of a chain of health clubs kills a business partner who was about to expose his crooked dealings. Making the death look like an unfortunate weightlifting accident, he cunningly creates the perfect alibi using a tape recording of his victim's voice - but he reckons without the intervention of the shabby sleuth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Robert Conrad and Philip Bruns

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: A plastic surgeon reports the disappearance of his wife, and Goren suspects there is more to the case than meets the eye, but struggles to find any flaws in the doctor's alibi. When body parts are found washed up on a beach, the detectives set a trap, placing a story in the papers claiming this could be the missing woman, to see how her husband reacts. Donna Hanover guest stars

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A 14-year-old girl is found murdered, and as detectives investigate the case, it is revealed she is involved in the same charitable foundation as four abduction victims. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

Starring Christopher Meloni, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: An infant ingests cocaine that has been mixed into baby food but Internal Affairs blocks the investigation, so the team goes undercover to expose a drug ring run by corrupt cops. programme.desc: Part two of two. Back at HQ, Abby creates a 3D computer model of the attack that occurred in Afghanistan and Joe dons a virtual-reality headset to relive the traumatic events. Will it jog his memory?

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Teenager Lydia Wade manages to escape a gang of masked men trying to abduct her and her friend Rosie, the daughter of a US Navy commander. Lydia's Navy captain father David Wade was shot dead during the attack and her mother is overseas, so Ziva offers to look after the girl, while Abby tries to jog her memory by creating facial reconstructions of the attackers based on some grainy CCTV footage. DiNozzo and McGee find links on Rosie's laptop to possible suspect John 'Diesel' Stevens

programme.title: Law & Order

programme.title: NCIS

Designer Charlie Luxton is tasked with making the cold and unsafe property a special haven for the family - and as always an army of local tradesmen turn out in force to help make the dream a reality

programme.title: The Flood (1963)
programme.desc: Eight children are trapped in a farmhouse by rising flood waters, but work together to find ways to survive until rescued. Drama, starring Waveney Lee and Ian Ellis

programme.desc: A hypochondriac is ordered by his doctor to avoid stressful situations. Unfortunately, he inadvertently becomes involved in an explosive international criminal caper. Comedy, starring Guy Middleton and Joan Winmill Brown

programme.title: Whistle Down the Wind (1961)
programme.desc: Three children become convinced that a bearded murderer taking refuge in the barn on their Lancashire farm is Jesus Christ, and do their utmost to protect him and conceal his presence from the outside world. programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: A couple whose children have flown the nest dream of starting life anew on the coast. They begin their search in Norfolk, where Seetha Hallett shows them round an an old post office, a Sixties house with an unusual roof and a property with an extensive garden - but will their stringent requirements be met and can they find room for their chickens and goats?

programme.title: Daddy Daycare
programme.desc: Nine men with limited childcare experience, who are either fathers or hope to have children soon, try to improve their skills by working in nurseries, taking on a variety of tasks such as nappy changing and supervising playtime. The first edition follows the efforts of three participants at a south London nursery - Garry, a workaholic whose wife thinks he does not have the patience to look after children, ex-military man Stefan, who takes a tough-love approach, and reluctant father-of-one Jay

programme.title: How to Stay Young
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary in which Angela Rippon and Dr Chris van Tulleken investigate the latest experiments and research that could help put the brakes on the ageing process. The first programme focuses on the physical aspects, revealing how much is down to how people live their lives and how people's lifestyle choices can help combat the effects of ageing. Chris finds out how public attitudes to the elderly can have a negative impact, spending a day as an 80-year-old with the help of Hollywood-style prosthetics, and Angela visits a study in Germany comparing the benefits of dancing with conventional gym exercises

programme.title: Unquiet Graves
programme.desc: Documentary exploring alleged collusion between the British government and loyalist death squads that murdered 120 civilians

programme.title: Jamal
programme.desc: A Rastafarian explains his philosophy whilst growing and cultivating an orchard on a small Caribbean island

programme.title: Strippers vs Werewolves (2012)
programme.desc: Lapdancer Justice kills a customer when he undergoes a terrifying transformation, and the body is quietly disposed of. The dead man was a werewolf, and at the next full moon, the other members of his pack plan to seek revenge. Justice and her fellow strippers have one month to prepare themselves for a battle to the death. Comedy horror, starring Adele Silva and Robert Englund

programme.title: Hot Property (2016)
programme.desc: A corporate spy loses her job after getting caught embezzling, and is no longer able to fund her lavish lifestyle. Faced with being evicted from her rented home, she puts all her skills of deception into fending off the landlord, replacement tenants and a psychotic estate agent. Comedy, starring MyAnna Buring and Tom Rhys Harries

programme.title: Cruel Passion (1977)
programme.desc: Two sisters are expelled from a nunnery and are plunged into a life of debauchery. While one embraces her new lifestyle, the other is desperate to cling to her old morality and sees a hope of salvation in a mysterious aristocrat who harbours a hidden dark side. Historical drama based on the novel Justine by the Marquis de Sade, starring Koo Stark and Martin Potter

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Dermot offers his body and Gary fixes up a burglar alarm in forlorn efforts to impress Deborah

programme.title: A Chorus of Disapproval (1988)
programme.desc: A shy widower gets entangled in romantic intrigue while rehearsing with an amateur theatrical troupe in Scarborough. Comedy based on Alan Ayckbourn's stage play, starring Jeremy Irons, Anthony Hopkins, Prunella Scales, Lionel Jeffries, Jenny Seagrove, Gareth Hunt and Patsy Kensit

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tour exploring Wapping for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Elizabeth and Her Children

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps a single mother who is keen to move her six-year-old son out of their cramped London flat to enjoy life by the sea. Seetha decides on the Isle of Wight as the perfect destination, and takes a look at a coastguard's cottage with stunning clifftop views, a modern mews house and a quirky beachfront property

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the story of the Second World War resistance movement set up around the coast by Winston Churchill to go into hiding and harass Nazi invaders should the Germans reach British soil

programme.title: Capital Conversation

programme.title: Opera Holland Park: Into the Light

programme.title: Hardware
programme.desc: Comedy series from the pen of Men Behaving Badly creator Simon Nye, following the trials and triumphs faced by the staff of Hamway's Hardware, a traditional DIY store manned by best friends Mike and Kenny, manager Rex and student Steve. A customer's suicide attempt convinces Mike he must try to be nicer to the clientele and Julie from the local cafe sets Steve up on a blind date. Starring Martin Freeman, with Peter Serafinowicz, Ken Morley, Ryan Cartwright and Ella Kenion

programme.title: Callan
programme.desc: Spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent

programme.title: The Best of Men
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about the birth of the Paralympics in 1948. Pioneering neurologist Dr Ludwig Guttmann arrives at Stoke Mandeville Hospital in Buckinghamshire and begins to transform the lives of his patients - Second World War servicemen with spinal cord injuries. He suggests using sport as a key part of their rehabilitation, and gets them involved in activities including wheelchair polo and basketball. Starring Eddie Marsan, Rob Brydon, George Mackay and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: Dying of the Light (2014)
programme.desc: A CIA agent is diagnosed with a degenerative condition that results in him being forced to retire from the world of intelligence. When he receives word that a terrorist who once tortured him has resurfaced, he goes rogue and sets out to assassinate the man while he is still able. Thriller, starring Nicolas Cage, Anton Yelchin and Irene Jacob

programme.title: Six Rounds
programme.desc: Among the 2011 London riots, a former boxer, must choose between his lower-class past plagued by crime or a new love and future in a middle-class society that he does not fit into

programme.title: Jamie's School Dinners
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver takes over the kitchen of Kidbrooke School in Greenwich, hoping to re-educate the junk food-loving children by introducing a healthy diet to improve concentration and eliminate long-term problems of obesity and heart disease. He joins forces with dinner lady Nora in his Feed Me Better campaign, aiming to promote nutritious dishes throughout the capital's schools before encouraging the rest of the country to follow suit

programme.title: Jamie's School Dinners
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver begins his campaign to encourage healthy eating at Kidbrooke School in Greenwich by teaching the children to cook, persuading a mother of nine to try his recipes and even dressing up as a giant sweetcorn. programme.desc: The lives of a wealthy British Indian family are turned upside down after some dark family secrets are revealed after the death of their father

programme.title: Jamie's School Dinners
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver's Feed Me Better campaign seems to be heading off the rails, with only 20 per cent of the teenagers eating his nutritious meals. With just six weeks to spread his message across the London borough of Greenwich, he decides to ban the junk food, making his healthy dishes the only choice, and whisk the 60 cooks away to army training camp to learn how to prepare dinners the Jamie way

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: The travellers move on to Switzerland still reeling from the previous day's events - but a visit to a local chocolate factory offering free samples improves their mood, and the spectacular view of Lake Lugano takes their breath away

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps two teachers from Milton Keynes find a retirement home on Wales' Lleyn Peninsula. After exploring the beautiful countryside, they examine six locations, including a converted chapel, a house overlooking the Menai Straits and the smallest property to appear in the series

programme.title: Winter Wipeout
programme.desc: Richard Hammond presents this chilly version of Total Wipeout, which sees contestants including a former world-class athlete and a binman wrapping up warm to tackle a snow-covered course with winter-themed obstacles and seasonal challenges. They must manoeuvre themselves past Granny's House, watching out for the angry dog nipping at their ankles and a front door guaranteed to smack them in the face. They then cling onto the ski lift to avoid plunging into the icy waters, before riding the conveyor belt that throws them onto those old Wipeout favourites, the big red balls. Amanda Byram laughs it up at the side of the course
